card_text: When you play this card, immediately Plow 1 field. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: You may not use a plow or the Harrow when you play this card.
In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Fishing" Action space, you receive 1 additional Food. From Round 8 onwards, you receive 2 additional Food.
Whenever you add a room to your Wooden hut, you only pay 2 Wood and 2 Reed.
card_rulings: If you build more than one room, you can use the Axe for each room.

This card sets a new base cost for a room. If you also have another card that sets a new base cost (e.g., Carpenter), you may only use one such card at the same time. If you build more than one room, you can choose the same or a different card for each room.
After you've used the Axe, you may then also use any card that changes the cost of a room (e.g., Clay Roof, Ladder, Stonecutter, and Wood Carver).

card_text: Whenever you use the "Bake bread" action, you can use the Baker's Oven to convert up to 2 Grain into 5 Food each. When you play this card, you can also take the "Bake bread" Action.
card_rulings: The old oven is returned: the Clay Oven and Stone Oven are returned to the major improvements, the Wood-fired Oven is removed from the game.
Is both an oven and a baking improvement.
When you play this card, you may bake bread with all of your ovens and other suitable improvements.
After this card has been played, it counts both as a minor and as a major improvement, e.g., for the requirements of other cards. (Of course, it still counts as a single improvement.)

card_text: The Clay Oven and Stone Oven are Minor Improvements for you. The Clay Oven, Stone Oven and Wood-fired Ovens cost you 1 building resource (of your choice) less.
You receive either 1 Wood or 1 Clay when you play this card. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

card_text: At any time, you can convert Grain to 2 Food (without having to Bake bread).
card_rulings: Using the Windmill does not count as baking.
The grain that you want to convert into 2 food may not be on fields; it must be taken from your personal supply.

card_text: When you Sow, you can plant Vegetables on this card as though it were a field. (This card does not count as a field when scoring.)
card_rulings: Vegetables on this card do count for the score at the end of the game.
When you sow on the Beanfield, you may use the Potato Dibber, Fieldsman, and Smallholder to plant extra vegetables.
If you have vegetable markers on the Bean Field, it counts as a prerequisite for the Herb Garden and the Strawberry Patch.
It is allowed to choose an action space with a sowing action, only to sow on the Beanfield.

card_text: Whenever you convert any 2 goods to Food at one time using a Fireplace, Cooking Hearth or Cooking Corner, you receive 1 additional Food.
card_rulings: If you convert 4 goods at once, you receive 2 additional food; for 6 goods, 3 additional food, etc.
Goods includes animals.
Baking bread counts as converting goods to food.

Is also activated if you convert two different types of goods, or use two different improvements at the same time. You may bake 1 bread and convert 1 other good at the same time; this also activates the  Gypsy's Crock.

card_text: At any time, you may convert goods to food as follows: Vegetable to 2 Food; Sheep to 1 Food; Wild boar to 2 Food; Cattle to 3 Food. Whenever you use the "Bake Bread" action, you may convert: Grain to 2 Food.
card_rulings: The  Simple Fireplace counts as a  Fireplace; for example, it can be upgraded to a  Cooking Hearth, and playing the  Simple Fireplace activates the  Charcoal Burner.
Is removed from the game when upgraded to a  Cooking Hearth.
You can own more than one  Fireplace.

With this card, you can convert several animals and vegetables to food at the same time. When you bake bread you can convert as many grain as you want. You may bake bread and convert other goods to food at the same time, in order to activate the  Gypsy's Crock.
Is a cooking improvement and a baking improvement, but not an oven.
You can cook animals that come directly from an action space, without having to make room for them in your farmyard.

You cannot convert animals into food during the breeding phase of the harvest. After the breeding phase of the last harvest, the game ends immediately; you can not convert the animals received in the last harvest to food at all.
This card never counts as a major improvement.

card_text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for each room in your Stone house. (In total, you receive 3 points instead of 2 per room.)(If you have played the Mansion, you do not score extra points for having the Half-timbered House.)
card_rulings: If you do not renovate to a stone house, there is no advantage.
The bonus points are in addition to extra points from, e.g., the Chief.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Fishing" Action space, you receive an additional 1 Food or 1 Reed.
At the end of the game, if your pastures occupy 6/7/8/9+ farmyard spaces, you receive 1/2/3/4 Bonus points.
card_rulings: The deciding factor is the number of fenced spaces in the farmyard. It is irrelevant how many pastures there are.

card_text: Place 2 Food on each remaining round space. At the start of each round, you receive the Food.
Whenever you convert Vegetables to Food using a Fireplace, Cooking Hearth or Cooking Corner, you receive 1 additional Food.
card_rulings: If you convert several vegetables to food at the same time, you receive 1 food for each vegetable.
Is not activated when you use a Cookhouse, Open Grill, or any other improvement, or when you convert vegetables to 1 food without an improvement.

card_text: Whenever you turn 1 Wood into Food using the Joinery, Sawmill or Cabinetmaker, you receive 1 additional Food. You can choose instead to turn a second Wood into exactly 2 Food.
Whenever you use the "Bake bread" action, you can use the Wood-fired Oven to turn any number of Grain into 3 Food each. When you play this card, you can also take the "Bake bread" action.
card_rulings: When you play this card, you may bake bread with all of your ovens and other suitable improvements.
Is an oven.

card_text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for a Clay hut, or 2 Bonus points for a Stone house.
card_rulings: This card is worth 2 points even if you receive bonus points for the Half-timbered House or the Mansion.
If you have the Chief's Daughter as well, you get points for both cards.

card_text: You can hold up to 2 additional Sheep in each of the pastures where you keep Sheep. You can keep up to 2 Sheep in each unfenced stable.
card_rulings: If you also have the Stablemaster, the Shepherd's Pipe has no effect on the unfenced stable for which the Stablemaster is used. However, it does affect all other unfenced stables.
Also increases the capacity of the Animal Yard and the Wildlife Reserve.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Fishing" Action space, you receive an additional 1 Food and 1 Reed.
Place 1 Food on each remaining odd-numbered Round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.
card_rulings: Only improvements and occupations that are open in front of you count.

card_text: Whenever you Sow fields with Vegetables, place 1 additional Vegetable on each field that you sow.
card_rulings: Additional vegetables are also placed on Beanfield, Turnip Field, and Lettuce Patch.

card_text: When you play this card, you receive 2 Food. From now, the Pottery is a Minor Improvement for you and costs you nothing.
card_rulings: You still have to take a minor improvement action to take the Pottery. You cannot take the Pottery if another player already has taken it.
The Pottery still counts as major improvement as well, e.g., for Nosebag or Administration.

card_text: Whenever you use a person's action to take Wood from an Action space, you can leave 2 of the Wood on the Action space and receive 3 Food in exchange.
card_rulings: If you also have the Mushroom Collector, you can use both cards to leave 3 wood to receive 5 food.
You may use this card only once per action.
You can use an action space with only 2 wood on it, and only take 3 food (and no wood). This still counts as taking wood, and activates, e.g., the Berry Picker, Wood Cart, and Pieceworker.
If there is less than 2 wood on the action space, for example because of the Wood Distributor, you may not use the Basket; not even if you receive additional wood from e.g. the Wood Cart.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Take 1 Grain" Action, you receive 1 additional Grain.
card_rulings: Corn Scoop is triggered when taking the "Take 1 Grain" Action Space.

card_text: You can replace 1 or 2 Reed with the same amount of Clay whenever you extend or renovate your home.
card_rulings: When building a room, you may choose to use 1 Reed and 1 Clay instead of the 2 Reed required.
Can be used for every room you build, if you build more than 1 room.

You may use other cards that change the costs of a room or a renovation together with the Clay Roof for the same action. These cards may even affect the clay cost introduced by the Clay Roof; e.g. with Clay Roof and Frame Builder you can add a room to your wooden hut for 6 wood.

card_text: Whenever you add a room to your Clay hut, you can pay 2 Clay, 1 Wood and 1 Reed instead of 5 clay and 2 Reed.
card_rulings: If you build more than one room at the same time, you may use the Clay Supports for all of the rooms. Alternatively, you may build some rooms for 5 clay & 2 reeds and other rooms for 2 clay, 1 wood, and 1 reeds.

This card sets a new base cost for clay rooms. If you also have another card that sets a new base cost (e.g., Clay Plasterer or Carpenter), you may only use one such card at the same time. However, if you build more than one room, you can choose different cards for each room.
After you've used the Carpenter, you may then also use any card that changes the cost of a room (e.g., Clay Roof, Ladder, and Bricklayer).

card_text: The Madonna Statue has no effect. (You must remove Improvements that are on the table in front of you. You may not discard cards from your hand. It is irrelevant whether you remove Major or Minor Improvements.)
card_rulings: If a discarded improvement would have given you something (resources, food, etc.) in a later round, you lose that claim.
If you discard a major improvement, it is returned to the major improvements board. If you discard a minor improvement, it is removed from the game.

card_text: When you play this card, you receive 1 Vegetable. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: The grain that you pay to play this card may not come from a field.
Activates the Market Woman when played, but not the Pieceworker.
You may not play this card without grain in your personal supply — not even in combination with the Market Woman.
In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

card_text: When you play this card, immediately Fence one space in your farmyard. (You do not need to pay Wood for the fences. If you already have a psture, this new one must be created next to it.) After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: Activates the Hedge Keeper, Farmer, Stablehand, and Animal Breeder when played.
The space must be unfenced before playing this card. It may contain a stable.

You must follow the usual rules for building fences: if you already have pastures, the new pasture must border an existing one. If you build more fences this turn, e.g., with the Hedge Keeper, you only check at the end of your turn if the pastures are legal.
In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

card_text: Whenever you Bake 1 or more Grain into bread, you receive 2 additional Food.
card_rulings: With the Baker, you receive at most 2 additional food from the Millstone in each harvest.
Is activated when you use a baking improvement (with the "baking" symbol) to convert grain to food.
Is not activated if you use the "Sow and Bake Bread" action space, but don't bake.
Bread is not baked if schnaps is distilled or beer is brewed, or when a Water Mill, Windmill or Hand Mill is used.

card_text: When you play this card, you receive either 1 Stone or 1 Reed. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

card_text: Place 1 Food on each remaining Round space for rounds 8 to 14. At the start of these rounds, you receive Food.
card_rulings: You do not receive food for the current round, or any of the earlier rounds.

card_text: The Outhouse has no effect. You can only build it if at least one other player has fewer than 2 Occupations. (It is irrelevant how many Occupations you have played.)
requirement: At least one other player has fewer than 2 occupations
card_rulings: In a 1-player game, you can only build the Outhouse if you have fewer than 2 occupations yourself.
The Academic counts as 2 occupations.

card_text: Place 1 Wood on each remaining even-numbered Round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Wood.
Place 1 Grain each on the spaces for rounds 5, 8, 11 and 14. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Grain.
card_rulings: You do not receive grain for the current round, or any of the earlier rounds.

card_text: On this card, you can plant Vegetables as you would on a field. Vegetables on this field can be converted to 4 Food when harvested. (This card does not count as a field when scoring.)
card_rulings: Vegetables on this card do count for the score at the end of the game.
To receive the 4 food for the harvested vegetable, you have to convert the vegetable to food immediately after harvesting.
You don't need a Fireplace or Cooking Hearth to convert vegetables harvested from this card into food.
When you sow on the Lettuce Patch, you may use the Potato Dibber, Fieldsman, and Smallholder to plant extra vegetables.
If you have vegetable markers on the Lettuce Patch, it counts as a prerequisite for the Herb Garden and the Strawberry Patch.
Using the Lettuce Patch to convert a vegetable to food does not activate the Spices.
It is allowed to choose an action space with a sowing action, only to sow on the Lettuce Patch.

card_text: Place 1 Reed each on the next 3 Round spaces. At the start of each round, you receive the Reed.
Whenever you use an "Occupation" Action, you may play 2 Occupations one after another. The second Occupation costs you 2 food.
card_rulings: You pay the normal cost for the first occupation.
If you also have the Bookshelf and/or the Patron, you receive food for both occupations that you play.
Is not activated when you use the "1 occupation or family growth" action space in the 5-player game, and you choose the family growth.
Is not activated when you don't use an action space to play an occupation (e.g., using the Scholar or Puppeteer).

card_text: You can renovate your Wooden hut to a Clay hut at any time without using a "Renovate" action. (You must still pay for the Renovation.)
card_rulings: You cannot renovate in the middle of an action, e.g., to use the clay received in an action to renovate to a clay hut, in order to profit from an improvement such as the Stump-Jump Plow in the same turn.
You may not use both the Conservator and the Builder's Trowel to renovate your wooden hut to a stone house out of turn.

card_text: Whenever you have 3/5 Sheep during the Field phase of a Harvest, you receive 1/2 additional Food.

When you play this card, immediately build 1 stable. (The stable does not cost you anything, but you must pay the cost shown to play this card.) After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

card_text: Whenever you have Sheep during the Field phase of a Harvest, you receive 1 Food for each third Sheep. Whenever you have Cattle during the Field phase, you receive 1 Food for each second Cattle.
Whenever you use the "Day Laborer" Action, you receive an additional 3 Stone.
card_rulings: The Quarry is triggered on the "Day Laborer" Action Space.
You receive an additional 2 stone.

card_text: When you play this card, immediately extend your Stone house by 1 room. (The room does not cost anything, but you must pay the costs shown to play this card.) After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: The new room is free. You only have to pay the costs of this minor improvement.

Cards that change the costs of minor improvements, such as Golden Hands, can be used to change the cost of the Stone House Extension. Cards that change the costs of a new room, such as Ladder or Frame Builder, cannot be used to change its cost.
Playing the Stone House Extension counts as building a room, and activates e.g. the Wet Nurse.
In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

card_text: Whenever you use one of the "1 Stone" Action spaces that become available in Stage 2 and Stage 4, you receive 1 additional Stone.
Place 1 Food on each remaining space for rounds 10 to 14. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.
card_rulings: You do not receive food for the current round, or any of the earlier rounds.

card_text: On this card you can hold up to two animals of your choice. They need not be the same type of animal. (This card does not count as a pasture for scoring.)(This card does not give you animals).
card_rulings: You do not receive animals from the general supply when you play this card.
The capacity of the Animal Yard is increased by the Drinking Trough.
If you also have the Shepherd's Pipe, you can keep 4 sheep or up to 3 sheep and 1 other animal on the Animal Yard.

card_text: Each pasture (with or without a stable) can hold up to 2 more animals.
card_rulings: Does not increase the capacity of an unfenced stable.

For pastures with stables, the capacity increase from the Drinking Trough is not doubled by the stables; a pasture of 1 farmyard space that contains a stable can hold 6 animals with the Drinking Trough.
Also increases the capacity of the Animal Yard and the Wildlife Reserve.

card_text: When you play this card, you receive 1 Cattle. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: The cattle may be immediately converted to food with a cooking improvement, Butcher or Meat-seller.
In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

card_text: Twice during the game, when you use either the "Plow 1 Field" or "Plow Field and/or Sow" action, you can Plow 3 fields instead of 1.
card_rulings: The Riding Plow is triggered on the "Plow 1 Field" and "Plow Field and/or Sow" Action Spaces.
You may choose to plow only 2 fields instead of 3 at once.
You can place two field tiles on this card when you play it, to show that you may use the plow 2 times.
This is a plow. Each time you use a plowing action space, you may only use one card out of the plows and the Harrow I68.

card_text: Once during the game, when you use either the "Plow 1 Field" or "Plow Field and/or Sow" action, you can Plow 3 fields instead of 1.
card_rulings: The Turnwrest Plow can be activated on either of the "Plow 1 Field" and "Plow Field and/or Sow" Action Spaces.
You may choose to plow only 2 fields instead of 3 at once.
You can place 1 field tile on this card when you play it, to show that you may use the plow 1 more time.
This is a plow. Each time you use a plowing action space, you may only use one card out of the plows and the Harrow.

card_text: During the Feeding Phase of each Harvest, you may trade 1 Vegetable for 1 of any type of Animal that you already have in your farmyard.
card_rulings: You may use this card only once per harvest.

card_text: Twice during the game, when you use the "Plow 1 Field" action, you may Plow 2 fields instead of 1. This does not apply to the "Plow 1 field and/or Sow" action.
card_rulings: The Moldboard Plow can only be triggered on the "Plow 1 Field" Action space.
You can place two field tiles on this card when you play it, to show that you may use the plow 2 times.
This is a plow. Each time you use the "Plow 1 Field" action space, you may only use one card out of the plows and the Harrow I68.

card_text: When you play this card, take 1 Food for each completed round of the game. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: The current round is not a "completed" round, not even if this card is played in the last action of the round.
In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.
Poor wording on the card: "take" here means "receive".

card_text: Whenever you use the "Bake bread" action, you can use the Baker's Kitchen to convert up to 2 Grain into 5 Food each. When you play this card, you can also take the "Bake bread" action.
card_rulings: Is a baking improvement, but not an oven. It may not be upgraded to a Bakehouse.
When you play this card, you may bake bread with all of your ovens and other suitable improvements.
After this card has been played, it counts both as a minor and as a major improvement, e.g., for the requirements of other cards. (Of course, it still counts as a single improvement.)

card_text: Place 1 Food each on the next 3 remaining Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.
card_rulings: The Well major improvement is returned to the major improvements board and may be bought again — even by the owner of the Village Well.

The Well gives 1 food per round for 5 rounds. These food remain on the board even when the 3 additional food from the Village Well are distributed, and are distributed again if the Well is purchased a second time.
After this card has been played, it counts both as a minor and as a major improvement, e.g., for the requirements of other cards. (Of course, it still counts as a single improvement.)

card_text: Whenever you use the "Plow 1 Field" or "Plow Field and/or Sow" action spaces, you can additionally take the "Bake bread" action.

Once during the game, when you use either the "Plow 1 Field" or the "Plow Field and/or Sow" action, you can Plow 2 fields instead of 1. Each other player can also do this once during the game, but must pay you 2 Food to do it.
card_rulings: The Harrow can only be triggered on the "Plow 1 Field" and "Plow Field and/or Sow" action spaces.
The Harrow is not a Plow.
Other players that use the Harrow may only plow 2 fields at once if they use one of the plowing action spaces.
The Harrow may not be combined with any of the plows.
You may not deny other players the right to use the Harrow.

card_text: Place 1 Food on each of the next 3 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.
card_rulings: Vegetable fields are fields on which there is at least 1 vegetable marker. An empty, harvested field does not count as a vegetable field.
The Beanfield, Lettuce Patch, and Turnip Field count as prerequisites for the Strawberry Patch, if there are vegetable markers on those cards.
The Seed Trader, Grocer, Giant Pumpkin, and Pumpkin Seed Oil do not count as prerequisites.

card_text: Whenever another player uses the Harrow or a Plow, you can immediately Plow 1 field as well. (If you use the Harrow or a Plow yourself, you do not get any advantage.)
card_rulings: The Punner is not a Plow.
If the other player does not plow more than 1 field on his action, you do not receive a field.

card_text: In Round 14, you cannot place any Family members.(Playing this card bans you from placing people - including a Guest - in Round 14.) Play this card at the latest during round 13. (Be prepared to play two Harvests in a row.)
card_rulings: The effects of this card are not optional! Prepare to play two harvests in a row.
This costs either 3 wood & 2 reeds or 3 clay & 2 reeds.
You may not use the Clay Roof, Brushwood Roof or Straw-thatched Roof when building the Holiday House.
You do not participate in the work phase (phase 3) in round 14, but you still can profit from the start-of-round phase (phase 1).
You may not place a guest in round 14 that you could otherwise have used.
You do not get points for the Church Warden if you have built the Holiday House.

card_text: Place 1 Food each on the next 4 remaining Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.

When you play this card, you receive a Guest token, which you may place once in the next round as you would a person. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: To represent the guest, you take a claim marker and turn it over to show the word "Guest". The token is used as a family member disc.
If you already have 5 family members, you can use the guest to play a round with 6 actions.
A guest is not counted in checking whether there is enough room in the hut or house.
The action performed by a guest counts for the Church Warden.
The guest does not need to be fed during harvest time.
A guest does not count as family member in the final scoring, even if you use the guest in the last round.
In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.
You can play this card in round 14, but you cannot use the guest marker you receive and it does not count in scoring.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Take 1 Grain" Action space, you receive 2 additional Grain.
During the Feeding phase of the Harvest, you can use the Hand Mill to turn either 1 Grain into 2 Food or 2 Grain into 4 Food.
card_rulings: Using the Hand Mill does not count as baking.
The grain that you want to convert into food may not be on fields; it must be taken from your personal supply.

card_text: At the end of the game, you receive 2 Bonus points if you have at least 5 fields. If you have played the Harrow, Punner, Yoke or a Plow, you require at least 6 fields.
card_rulings: Fields on cards, such as Beanfield and Forester, do not count. Fields with more than one stack of grain because of the Scarecrow count as only 1 field.
If you also played a plow, Harrow, Punner, or Yoke, but you discarded it for the Madonna Statue, they do not count for the Rake.
If you played more than one of the listed improvements, you still need only 6 fields.

card_text: Whenever you Fence a new pasture that covers at least 4 farmyard spaces, take 2 Sheep and place them in the new pasture.
card_rulings: If you divide a pasture of more than 4 spaces into several smaller pastures, these do not count as a new pasture, unless the number of pastures of at least 4 spaces increases.
If you fence 2 large pastures in one action, you receive 4 sheep.
The word "take" should here be read as "receive".

card_text: When you Sow, you can plant up to 2 Wood on this card, as shown. The Wood is treated the same as sown Grain and is harvested during the Field phase. (This card does not count as a field when scoring.)
card_rulings: If you receive for example 4 grain on your fields when sowing, you can also receive 4 wood once or twice on the Copse card.
The Smallholder may not be used to plant extra wood.
In each harvest, you take one wood from each of the stacks.
It is allowed to choose an action space with a sowing action, only to sow wood on the Copse.
Wood on this card does not count for the Storehouse Clerk. At the end of the game, it does count for the Joinery or the Sawmill.
The Copse is not considered to be adjacent to any farmyard space or other card, regardless of its location, and does not benefit from the Planter Box.

card_text: Whenever you use a person to take Wood that is on an Action space, you receive 2 additional Wood.
card_rulings: Is not activated when you use the "1 Reed, Stone, and Wood" action space in the 5-player game.

If you use an action space that contains wood, and you leave all the wood on the action space because of Basket, Mushroom Collector, or Pig Catcher, you still receive the wood from the Wood Cart, but (if you have more than one of those cards) you cannot leave wood received from the Wood Cart on the action space to use another of those cards.

card_text: Whenever another player uses the "3 Wood" Action space, s/he must give you one of the Wood. (This does not apply to the "4 Wood" Action space in the 5-player game.)
card_rulings: When you demand the 1 wood from another player, the other player is entitled to change his mind and select a different action instead — it is easy to overlook the Spinney.
You only get 1 wood, regardless of the amount of wood currently on the action space.
The Spinney is activated before the other player's Basket, Mushroom Collector, or Pig Catcher, or any player's Wood Buyer.

card_text: When you play this card, immediately extend your Wooden hut by 1 room. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: The new room is free. You only have to pay the costs of this minor improvement.

Cards that change the costs of minor improvements, such as Golden Hands, can be used to change the cost of the Wooden Hut Extension. Cards that change the costs of a new room, such as Ladder or Frame Builder, cannot be used to change its cost.
Playing the Wooden Hut Extension counts as building a room, and activates, e.g., the Wet Nurse.
When using the "Family Growth and Minor Improvement" action space, it is not possible to build this improvement first, and then use the new room to grow your family in.
In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

card_text: Whenever you use one of the "Stone" Action spaces that are placed in stage 2 and 4, you receive 1 additional Stone. If you pay 1 Food, you can take 2 Stone instead of 1.
card_rulings: Is not activated when you use any other action space to take stone.

card_text: The player with the most valuable street receives 2 Bonus points when scoring. (The Paved Road that costs 5 Stone is more valuable than the Clay Path that costs 3 Clay which is more valuable than this Wooden Path.)
card_rulings: Even if the other streets have also been played, only one bonus of 2 points is be awarded per game (not 2 bonus points per played street).

card_text: Place 1 Food each on the next 8 remaining Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.
card_rulings: May be built with either 2 wood & 1 reeds or with 2 clay & 1 reeds.

card_text: At any time, you may convert goods to Food as follows: Vegetables to 4 Food; Sheep to 2 food; Wild boar to 3 Food; Cattle to 4 Food. Whenever you use the "Bake bread" action, you may convert: Grain to 3 Food.
card_rulings: If you upgrade the Cooking Hearth major improvement, the card is returned to the major improvements board. If the minor improvement Cooking Hearth is upgraded, it is removed from the game.
Is a cooking improvement and a baking improvement, but not an oven.
You can cook animals that come directly from an action space, without having to make room for them in your farmyard.

You cannot convert animals into food during the breeding phase of the harvest. After the breeding phase of the last harvest, the game ends immediately; you can not convert the animals received in the last harvest to food at all.
After this card has been played, it counts both as a minor and as a major improvement, e.g. for the requirements of other cards. (Of course, it still counts as a single improvement.)

card_text: Whenever you have empty Fields after the Field phase of the Harvest, you can sow Grain in them immediately. Place 1 fewer Grain from the Supply on these fields.
card_rulings: Is built with either 2 wood & 2 reeds or 2 clay & 2 reeds.
Is used during the harvest at the same time as the Water Mill and before the Harvest Helper.
Activates the Field Worker when used.
The effect of the Corn Storehouse is optional. You may choose to sow fewer fields than you're able to sow.
You may use the Corn Storehouse to sow on the Acreage, but not on fields for other goods than wood (e.g., Copse and Forester).
Activates the Scarecrow when used, but not the Scythe.

card_text: Whenever the Well is built or upgraded to a Village Well, you receive 4 Food, and the other players receive 1 Food each. (If the Well has already been built, everyone receives the food when the Flagon is played.)
card_rulings: If the Well is rebuilt after the Village Well upgrade, the food is distributed again.
If the Village Well is already on the table when the Flagon is played, the food is not distributed in double quantities.
If both the Village Well and the Well are on the table when the Flagon is played, the double amount of food is distributed immediately.

card_text: You can place exactly two people immediately after one another, if at least one of them uses a "Wild Boar", "Cattle" or "Sheep" Action space. (This does not apply to the "Either 1 Sheep and 1 Food or 1 Wild boar..." Action space from the 5-player game.)
card_rulings: If you place 2 people the first time it's your turn in the work phase, you may play your third person the second time; you do not have to skip a turn.
You cannot place more than two people immediately after one another turn with the Lasso.
If you have 4 or more people, you may use the Lasso more than once in the same round.
You can use the animal action space with your first or second family member.
When you play this card, you can immediately use it to place another family member on one of the three animal action spaces.
Using the Lasso counts as two turns.

card_text: The player with the most valuable street receives 2 Bonus points when scoring. (The Paved Road that costs 5 Stone is more valuable than this Clay Path which is more valuable than the Wooden Path that costs 1 Wood.)
card_rulings: Even if the other streets have also been played, only one bonus of 2 points is be awarded per game (not 2 bonus points per played street).
The bonus points are in addition to the 1 normal point you get for playing this card.

card_text: Whenever you Sow, each of the fields you sow that is orthogonally adjacent to a room in your home gets an additional 2 Grain or 1 Vegetable.
card_rulings: You get 2 extra Grain on each Grain field and 1 extra Vegetable on each Vegetable field.
Not optional; you must plant the extra grain and vegetables.
When a room is built next to a field that has already been sown, you do not get additional goods until the next time you sow the field.
If a field contains 2 stacks because of the Scarecrow, both stacks get an extra grain.

Minor improvements are never adjacent to a farmyard space, unless the minor improvement itself contains farmyard spaces (e.g. Moor Enclosures). Improvements such as Acreage and Copse do not benefit from the Planter Box, not even from an improvement that is used as a room (e.g., Reed Hut).

card_text: You need 1 less Reed to extend or renovate your home or to build the Water Mill, Half-timbered House, Chicken Coop, Holiday House, Mansion, or Corn Storehouse.
card_rulings: Can be used for every room you build, if you build more than 1 room.
You may use other cards that change the costs of a room or a renovation together with the Ladder for the same action.
The Ladder has no effect if the number of reeds required is already lowered to 0, e.g. by using both Clay Roof and Frame Builder when building a new room.

card_text: At the end of each round that does not end with a Harvest, you can (optionally) take 1 Grain or Vegetable from each of your fields and place it in your personal supply.
card_rulings: The effect of the Manure is optional, but if you harvest, you must harvest all of your fields.

You have a field phase (harvest phase 1) after each round, except that outside a regular harvest the Milking Shed, Spindle, Butter Churn, Milking Stool, Loom, and Milking Hand do not earn any additional food.
Using the Manure activates the Corn Storehouse, Water Mill (only for you), and Gardener, but not the Cube Cutter.
Improvements, such as the House Goat and Horse, do not count as animals for the requirement to play this card.
You also harvest the Copse, Forester, and Vineyard.

card_text: In each Harvest, at the beginning of the Field phase, count the total number of Sheep and Cattle in all the players' farms. You receive 1 Food for each fifth Sheep and for each third Cattle.
card_rulings: The total number of sheep and cattle is counted.
Pets and live animals on improvement cards are also counted.
Animals on the Tanner and the Taxidermist do not count.
At harvest time, the Milking Shed is processed before the Spindle, Butter Churn, Milking Stool, and Loom.

card_text: The player with the most valuable street receives 2 Bonus points when scoring. (This Paved Road is more valuable than the Clay Path that  costs 3 Clay which is more valuable than the Wooden Path that costs 1 Wood.)
card_rulings: This Paved Road is more valuable than the Clay Path, which is more valuable than the Wooden Path.
This card always scores 2 bonus points. Even if the other streets have also been played, only one bonus of 2 points is be awarded per game (not 2 bonus points per played street).
The bonus points are in addition to the 2 normal points you get for playing this card.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Fishing" Action space or receive Reed on an Action space, you receive 1 additional Food.
card_rulings: Is activated when you use an action space on which reed is placed each round. You cannot use such an action space just to use this card if the action space contains no reed (e.g. when you have the Bureaucrat.)
Is activated by the action space "Take 1 Building Resource" in 3-player game if you take reed.
Is also activated by the action space "1 Reed, Stone, and Wood" in 5-player game and the action space "1 Reed, Stone, and Food" in 4-player game.
Is not activated when you play the Reed Exchange or Helpful Neighbours, or use the Reed Buyer, Reed Collector, or any other occupation or improvement to get reed.
Is not activated when you use the Net Fisherman to collect the food from the "Fishing" action space.

card_text: When you play this card, you receive 2 Reed. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: You may not exchange 1 wood or 1 clay for only 1 reed, or exchange 1 wood and 1 clay for 2 reeds.
Playing the Reed Exchange does not activate the Net Fisherman.
In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

card_text: Whenever another player converts 1 or more animals to food, you receive 1 Food from the supply. During the Feeding phase of the Harvest, you are the last player to take a turn. (so you can benefit if other players slaughter).(If you slaughter, you receive no advantage.)
card_rulings: If both the Slaughterhouse and the Slaughterman are on the table, the owners of these two cards feed their families in current turn order (which is the turn order of the next round).

card_text: During the Feeding phase of the Harvest, you can use the Schnaps Distillery to convert at most 1 Vegetable into 4 Food. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point each for your 5th and 6th Vegetables.
card_rulings: The vegetable that is used to buy the Schnaps Distillery and vegetables that you want to convert into 4 food may not be on fields; they must be taken from your personal supply.
Distilling schnaps does not count as baking and does not activate the Spices.

card_text: You no longer need Reed when you extend or renovate your home.
card_rulings: Grain fields are fields or improvement cards on which there is at least 1 grain marker. An empty, harvested field does not count as a grain field.
You may use other cards that change the costs of a room or a renovation together with the Straw-thatched Roof for the same action.

card_text: The Tavern is an additional Action space. Whenever another player uses it, s/he receive 3 Food. Whenever you use it, you can choose either to take 3 Food or to score 2 Bonus points. (If another player uses the Tavern, you do not receive anything from it.)
card_rulings: As on every other action space, at most one person may use the Tavern in each round.
If you take this action yourself and you choose the bonus points, write them on the scoring pad.
The Tavern counts as played improvement, e.g., for the Village Elder or as prerequisite for the Carp Pond.

card_text: Immediately before scoring, you receive 1 additional animal of each type that you already have, if you have space in your pastures. (This does not apply to the House Goat and the Horse).
card_rulings: It does apply to horses (that are not minor improvements).
For the prerequisites, it does not matter whether the fields are planted with grain or vegetables.
The Beanfield, Lettuce Patch, and Turnip Field count as prerequisites for the Animal Feed, if there are vegetable markers on those cards.
The Acreage counts for the number of fields planted on it.
The Copse, Giant Pumpkin, and Forester do not count as prerequisites.
You may return animals to the general supply in order to make room for the new arrivals.
As the game is over at this point, you may not convert the new animal to food, for example to pay for the Hide Farmer.
If you also have played the Tenant Farmer, Animal Feed is used before returning the borrowed animals.

card_text: This card can hold up to 1 Sheep, 1 Wild boar and 1 Cattle. (This card does not count as a pasture when scoring.)
card_rulings: The capacity of the Wildlife Reserve is increased by the Shepherd's Pipe and the Drinking Trough.

card_text: After the Field phase of a Harvest, each player can use the Water Mill to convert up to 1 Grain to 3 Food. Each player that uses the Water Mill must give you 1 of the Food.
card_rulings: Using the Water Mill does not count as baking.
You may use the Water Mill yourself for free.
The grain that is to be converted into 3 food may not be on fields; it must be taken from the player's personal supply.

card_text: When you play this card, you receive 2 Vegetables. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: The grain that you pay for the vegetables may not come from a field.
Activates the Market Woman when played, but not the Pieceworker.
In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

card_text: This card is an additional Action space. A player who uses this action space must pay you 1 Food and receives 5 Clay. If you use the Clay Deposit yourself, you may choose to take 2 Bonus points instead of the Clay.
card_rulings: If you use the Clay Deposit yourself, you do not need to have or to pay any food.
The Clay Deposit counts as played improvement, e.g., for the Village Elder or as prerequisite for the Carp Pond.

card_text: When you Sow, you can plant Grain on this card. (This card does not count as a field when scoring.)
card_rulings: The Acreage does not count as a field when scoring.
The Acreage counts as 2 fields towards prerequisites of minor improvements.
You may choose to only sow one grain on this card. (You may still sow another grain in a later sowing action.)
In each harvest, you take one grain from each of the stacks.
If you receive 4 grain on fields when sowing because of occupations such as Fieldsman and Smallholder, you also receive 4 grain in each stack on the Acreage.
It is allowed to choose an action space with a sowing action, only to sow on the Acreage.
The Acreage is not considered to be adjacent to any farmyard space or other card, regardless of its location, and does not benefit from the Planter Box.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Bake bread" Action, you can use the Bakehouse to convert up to 2 Grain into 5 Food each. When you play this card, you can also take the "Bake bread" Action.
card_rulings: The old oven is returned: the Clay Oven and Stone Oven are returned to the major improvements, the Wood-fired Oven and Baker's Oven are removed from the game. The Baker's Kitchen may not be upgraded to a Bakehouse.
Is a baking improvement, but not an oven.
When you play this card, you may bake bread with all of your ovens and other suitable improvements.
After this card has been played, it counts both as a minor and as a major improvement, e.g., for the requirements of other cards. (Of course, it still counts as a single improvement.)

card_text: When you play this card, you receive 3 Wood. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

card_text: Place 2 Food on each remaining even-numbered Round space. At the start of these rounds, you take the Food.
card_rulings: Only the improvements and occupations that lie open in front of you count.
Discarded improvements (e.g., because of upgrading or playing Madonna Statue) do not count.
"Take" should here be interpreted as "Receive".

card_text: Whenever you convert at least 1 animal to Food during the Feeding phase of the Harvest, you receive 1 additional Food.
card_rulings: You get a maximum of 1 food per harvest.

card_text: During the feeding phase of the Harvest, you can use the Brewery to convert at most 1 Grain to 3 Food. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for your ninth Grain.
card_rulings: The grain that is used to buy the Brewery and grain that you want to convert into 3 food may not be on fields; they must be taken from your personal supply.
Brewing beer does not count as baking.

card_text: Whenever you play an Occupation, you may also take the "Bake bread" action.
card_rulings: Is activated when you use the Puppeteer or Educator.
When you play more than one occupation in one action, you can bake bread more than once too.
You can also bake bread if you play an occupation during the turn of another player.
You may not use the food from the baking to pay for the occupation.

card_text: Whenever you play 1 Occupation, you receive 3 Food before you pay the costs of the Occupation.
card_rulings: If you also have the Patron, you can use both to receive 5 food for each occupation you play.
When used with the Writing Desk to play two occupation cards, the additional 3 food is distributed twice.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Plow 1 Field" or "Plow Field and/or Sow" action spaces, you can additionally take the "Bake bread" action.
Place 1 Food on each of the next 3 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.
Twice during the game, when you use the "Plow 1 Field" action space, you can plow 3 fields instead of 1. The Swing Plow cannot be used with the "Plow 1 Field and/or Sow" action.
card_rulings: The Swing Plow can only be activated when you use the "Plow 1 Field" action space.
You may choose to plow only 2 fields instead of 3 at once.
You can place two field tiles on this card when you play it, to show that you may use the plow 2 times.
This is a plow. Each time you use the "Plow 1 Field" action space, you may only use one card out of the plows and the Harrow I68.

card_text: Place 1 Grain on each remaining space for rounds 8, 10 and 12. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Grain.
card_rulings: You may not build the Granary with 2 wood & 1 clay or with 1 wood & 2 clay.
Taking the grain does not activate the Grain Cart, Corn Scoop, Pieceworker, Sycophant, Seed Seller, Greengrocer, Market Crier, or Field Watchman.
You do not receive grain for the current round, or any of the earlier rounds.

card_text: Add 4 and 7 to the current Round and place 1 Vegetable on each corresponding Round space. At the start of these rounds, you can pay 1 Food to take the Vegetable.
card_rulings: If you do not wish to pay the 1 food to buy the vegetables, the vegetable is returned to the general supply.
Food that you receive at the start of a round (e.g., because of the Well) can be used to pay for the vegetable immediately.
You must pay the food before you take the vegetable.

card_text: Whenever you Sow, place 1 additional Grain or Vegetable from the general supply on your newly planted fields.
card_rulings: Fields that have been already planted when the card is played will only benefit from the Liquid Manure when they are emptied and replanted.
You can only put an extra grain on grain fields, and an extra vegetable on vegetable fields.
You also place extra grain or vegetables when using the Corn Storehouse.
The additional grain or vegetable from the Liquid Manure is added to additional goods from the Potato Dibber, Planter Box, Fieldsman, and Smallholder.
You also place extra grain or vegetables on the Beanfield, Turnip Field, and Lettuce Patch.
If you plant wood on the Copse or Forester, you place 1 additional wood for each wood you plant. You also plant additional food on the Vineyard.
Improvements, such as the House Goat and Horse, do not count as animals for the requirement to play this card.

card_text: Once during the game, when you use the "Plow 1 Field" action, you can Plow 3 fields instead of 1. This does not apply to the "Plow 1 field and/or Sow" action.
card_rulings: The Crooked Plow is triggered only on the "Plow 1 Field" Action Space.
You may choose to plow only 2 fields instead of 3 at once.
You can place 1 field tile on this card when you play it, to show that the plow may be used 1 more time.
This is a plow. Each time you use the "Plow 1 Field" action space, you may only use one card out of the plows and the Harrow.

card_text: In each Feeding phase, you receive 1 Food. Apart from the House Goat, you cannot hold any other animal in your home. (even if you have the Animal Tamer.)(You cannot choose to let the House Goat run free to make room for a different animal in your house.)
card_rulings: If you have the House Goat, the Animal Tamer has no effect.
You may place 1 food on this card for each remaining harvest, to ensure that you do not forget to take the food.
The House Goat does not count as an animal, e.g., for prerequisites of minor improvements.
If you already have played the Sheepdog, you cannot play the House Goat, and vice versa.

card_text: The next stable you place in your farmyard, as well as your 3rd, 6th, 9th, 12th and 15th fence, costs you nothing. (You can only place fences if they enclose a complete pasture.)
card_rulings: If you build free fences in another way (e.g., with the Hedge Keeper) you may not save the free fences from the Sawhorse for another turn.
Free fences from the Sawhorse are built before the free fences from the Hedge Keeper.

card_text: Each Harvest, you may convert up to 1 Wood to 3 Food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 Bonus points for 2/4/5 Wood. (A repurchased Joinery does not give any additional Bonus points, but can be used to convert an additional 1 Wood each Harvest.)
card_rulings: If you have both the Sawmill and the Joinery at the end of the game, you do not get any bonus points for wood from the Joinery.
After the Joinery has been upgraded, it may be bought again by any player.
At the end of the game, wood on the Copse and the Forester counts. Wood on the Basin Maker and Resource Seller does not count.
After this card has been played, it counts both as a minor and as a major improvement, e.g. for the requirements of other cards. (Of course, it still counts as a single improvement.)

card_text: At the end of the game, you get 2 Bonus points if your home contains 5 rooms, or 4 Bonus points if you have 6 or more rooms.
When you play this card, you can immediately Plow 1 field for each Plow that has been played (by any player) and 1 field for the Harrow, if it has been played.
card_rulings: You may choose to plow fewer fields.
If a plow or Harrow has been discarded, e.g., for the Madonna Statue, it does not count.

card_text: Discard all the remaining Minor improvements in your hand, and draw 7 new Minor Improvements. You can play 1 more Minor Improvement immediately. (You must pay the costs of the new Improvement and, where appropriate, meet the conditions for playing it.)
card_rulings: If the players are only playing with the complex (K) deck of cards, the new cards are also drawn from the complex deck.
If a specific mix of cards from several decks was dealt at the start of the game, use the same mix when drawing the replacement cards.

The improvements are drawn from the cards that were removed from the game at the start. This does not include cards that were discarded during the game, e.g., for upgrades or to play the Madonna Statue.

card_text: Whenever you receive Reed on an Action space, you receive an additional 2 Food. This is reduced to 1 Food if you receive other building resources as well as Reed.
card_rulings: Is activated when you use an action space on which reed is placed each round. You cannot use such an action space just to use this card if the action space contains no reed (e.g., when you have the Bureaucrat Č07.)
Is activated by the action space "Take 1 Building Resource" in 3-player game if you take reed.
Is also activated by the action space "1 Reed, Stone, and Wood" in 5-player game and the action space "1 Reed, Stone, and Food" in 4-player game, but you receive only 1 food.
Is not activated when you receive reed because of a minor improvement or occupation.
If the Wood Distributor placed wood on the "1 Reed" action space, that space only provides 1 additional food.
Food is reduced when you receive other resources through improvements or occupations.
Other building resources are wood, clay, and stone.

card_text: Whenever you use a "Family growth" action (or already have a person on that space when you play the Clapper), place 1 additional Grain on any of your fields that already contains at least 1 grain.
card_rulings: Take the grain from the general supply.
If there is more than one stack of grain on a field, on the Acreage or because of the Scarecrow, you place 1 grain on each of the stacks.
Is not activated when you use the Wet Nurse, Village Beauty, or Lover.
If you use the Mother of Twins for the same action, you still only get 1 additional grain per field.

In the 5-player game, there is an action space that gives players a choice of actions, including family growth. From round 5 onwards, the Clapper is activated by using this space, even if you choose the other action.
Also place additional wood on the Copse or the Forester, and additional food on the Vineyard.
Using the Clapper does not count as sowing.

card_text: At any time, you can convert goods to Food as follows: Vegetables to 3 Food; Sheep to 2 Food; Wild boar to 3 Food; Cattle to 4 Food. Whenever you use the "Bake bread" action, you may convert: Grain to 3 Food.
card_rulings: If you upgrade the Fireplace major improvement, the card is returned to the major improvements board. If the minor improvement Simple Fireplace is upgraded, it is removed from the game.
You can own more than one Cooking Hearth.

With this card, you can convert several animals and vegetables to food at the same time. When you bake bread you can convert as many grain as you want. You may bake bread and convert other goods to food at the same time, in order to activate the Gypsy's Crock.
Is a cooking improvement and a baking improvement, but not an oven.
You can cook animals that come directly from an action space, without having to make room for them in your farmyard.

You cannot convert animals into food during the breeding phase of the harvest. After the breeding phase of the last harvest, the game ends immediately; you can not convert the animals received in the last harvest to food at all.
Is identical in all respects to the major improvement Cooking Hearth, except that it can be played as a minor improvement, and that it cannot be paid for with clay.
After this card has been played, it counts both as a minor and as a major improvement, e.g., for the requirements of other cards. (Of course, it still counts as a single improvement.)

card_text: When you play this card, take 1 Grain. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.
The word "take" should be interpreted as "receive".

card_text: Place 1 Food on each of the next 5 Round spaces. At the start of each round, you receive the Food.
card_rulings: Vegetable fields are fields on which there is at least 1 vegetable marker. An empty, harvested field does not count as a vegetable field.
The Beanfield, Lettuce Patch, and Turnip Field count as prerequisites for the Strawberry Patch, if there are vegetable markers on those cards.
The Seed Trader, Grocer, Giant Pumpkin, and Pumpkin Seed Oil do not count as prerequisites.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Day Laborer" Action space, you receive 3 additional Clay.
When you play this card, immediately extend your Clay hut by 1 room. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: The new room is free. You only have to pay the costs of this minor improvement.

Cards that change the costs of minor improvements, such as Golden Hands Č13, can be used to change the cost of the Clay Hut Extension. Cards that change the costs of a new room, such as Ladder or Frame Builder, cannot be used to change its cost.
Playing the Clay Hut Extension counts as building a room, and activates, e.g., the Wet Nurse.
In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

card_text: Whenever you have at least 1/3/5 Cattle during the Field phase of a Harvest, you receive 1/2/3 Food. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for every 2 Cattle.
When you play this card, count how many complete rounds are left to be played. You can Plow this many Fields, up to a maximum of 3.
card_rulings: The Ox Team is not a plow.
You may choose to plow fewer fields.

card_text: You receive 2 Bonus points for any one type of animal missing from your farm at the end of the game. (The horse replaces this type of animal.)(You still lose the point for the missing animal type.)
card_rulings: If you are missing several types of animal, you still only get 2 bonus points from the Horse, and you still receive 1 minus point for every missing type of animal.
The score for the missing type of animal still counts as negative for the Constable and Yeoman Farmer.
The Horse does not need to be placed in the farmyard.
The Horse does not count as an animal for prerequisites of minor improvements.
The Horse does not count as a horse, but horses can be the type of animal for which the Horse gives you points.

card_text: Whenever you extend or renovate your home, you can replace 1 or 2 Reed with the same amount of Wood.
card_rulings: Can be used for every room you build, if you build more than 1 room.
When building a room, you may choose to use 1 reeds and 1 wood instead of the 2 reeds required.

You may use other cards that change the costs of a room or a renovation together with the Brushwood Roof for the same action. For example, if you also have the Clay Roof, you may use both to replace 2 reeds with 1 wood and 1 clay.

card_text: When you Sow, you can plant Vegetables on this card as you would on a field. When you play this card, you can also take the "Sow" action. (This card does not count as a field when scoring.)
card_rulings: You may use the sowing action for all of your empty fields.
Vegetables on this card do count for the score at the end of the game.
Whenever you sow on the Turnip Field, you may use the Potato Dibber, Fieldsman, and Smallholder to plant extra vegetables.
If you have vegetable markers on the Turnip Field, it counts as a prerequisite for the Herb Garden and the Strawberry Patch.
It is allowed to choose an action space with a sowing action, only to sow on the Turnip Field.

card_text: Place one family member token that you have not yet brought into the game onto this card, where it will live for the rest of the game. It can take actions and must be fed, but it is not worth any points while living in the Reed Hut.
card_rulings: The new person can be used in the current round, as it is put in "its own room" immediately when playing this card.
The occupant of the Reed Hut is placed after your family members and the guest from the Guest minor improvement, but before the guest received from the Keg.
Playing the Reed Hut does not count as a family growth action.
The person in the Reed Hut is not counted when checking if family growth is allowed. They do not count as part of the family.

You can move this person into your home with a family growth action later. That family growth action must be taken by another family member. The former occupant of the Reed Hut counts as newborn, and may not take an action that round, unless you use the Adoptive Parents.
You can play this card even if you do not have a family member token left in your supply, but you don't get an extra person.

If you discard the Reed Hut because of the Madonna Statue, you must discard the occupant of the Reed Hut immediately if you haven't yet placed it this round. If you've already placed the occupant of the Reed Hut, discard it in the return home phase of this round.

card_text: You can use any "Family Growth" Action space, even if another player has already placed a person there.
card_rulings: This works for all spaces that include a family growth action.

In the 5-player game, there is an action space that gives players a choice of actions, including family growth. From round 5 onwards, you may always use this space, even if you choose the other action.

Grain fields are fields on which there is at least 1 grain marker. An empty, harvested field does not count as a grain field. Fields with other resources or food, such as Copse and Vineyard, do not count.
The Acreage and fields on which a second grain has been planted using the Scarecrow count for the number of grain planted on them.
You cannot use the action space if it is occupied by one of your own family members.

card_text: Place 1 Food each on the next 5 remaining Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.
When you play this card, take 1 Wild boar. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: The wild boar may be immediately converted to food with a cooking improvement, Butcher or Meat-seller.
In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.
The word "take" should here be interpreted as "receive".

card_text: Place 1 Stone on each remaining even-numbered Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Stone.
When you play this card, you receive 2 Stone. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: You may not exchange 1 wood or 1 clay for only 1 stone, or exchange 1 wood and 1 clay for 2 stone.
In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

card_text: At the end of the game, you receive 2 Bonus points for each room in your Stone house. (In total, you receive 4 points per room instead of the usual 2 points.)
card_rulings: If you also have the Half-timbered House, you only get the bonus points from the Mansion.
If you also have the Chief, you get the bonus points for both cards.

card_text: This card can hold an unlimited number of Wild boar. (This card not count as a pasture when scoring.)
card_rulings: The wild boar on this card are included when scoring wild boar.

card_text: Whenever you have at least 1/4/7 Sheep during the Field phase of a Harvest, you receive 1/2/3 Food. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for every 3 Sheep.

For each Animal that you slaughter and return to the general supply, you may place 1 Food from your personal supply in 1 of your rooms. You may have a maximum of 1 Food in each room. You cannot use these Food any more, but each is worth 1 Bonus point at the end of the game.
card_rulings: You can take the food from your supply after you've received the food from slaughtering the animal.
If you also have the Tanner, Basin Maker, Taxidermist, and/or Brush Maker, you may use those cards as well as the Pelts for each converted animal.

card_text: When you play this card, immediately take the Field and Breeding phases of a Harvest on your farmyard. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: Activates all your cards that can be used during the field and breeding phases of a harvest.
Activates the Water Mill (only for you), also if owned by another player.
Does not activate the Layabout.

card_text: Once per round, you may return 1 of your animals to the supply for a guest token, which you place this round as you would a family member. (The guest represents the animal.)
card_rulings: You can use the Animal Farm at the latest when it's your next turn after the one in which you placed your last family member (including the occupant of the Reed Hut and other guests except for the one from the Keg).
If another player has the Robber, Animal Farm does not count as a person left to place, unless you already took the guest token.
You can exchange an animal that comes directly from an action space or from an improvement or occupation, without having to make room for it in your farmyard.

If you discard the Animal Farm with the Madonna Statue before you've used the guest marker, you have to discard that guest marker without using it. If you've already placed the guest marker, it is discarded in the return home phase as usual.
Requirement 2 animal types, except horse. You may return 1 of your animals to the supply for a guest token, except horse.
The guest may be taken before or during your last family member placing.

card_text: When you sow, you can plant grain on this card as you would on a field. When harvesting, you must immediately bake this field's harvested grain.
card_rulings: You receive a baking action that you can and must use for this grain only.
The grain still counts as harvested, e.g., for Bountiful Harvest and Grain Market.
Every grain that is moved from this field to your personal supply has to be baked, including when Manure, Almanac, Harvest Feast, or Fish Farm are used.
Grain moved elsewhere (e.g., to another player's supply by Harvest Helper) cannot be baked immediately.
When you harvest more than 1 grain at the same time, e.g. with the Harvest Feast, you must bake all grain in 1 baking action.
If you cannot bake a grain, e.g., because you discarded your baking implement for the Madonna Statue, you must convert it to 1 food.
If you also have the Baker, you get two separate baking actions during the harvest; you may use the same baking implement for both.
This card does not count as a field when scoring.
It counts as a field towards prerequisites of minor improvements.
It is allowed to choose an action space with a sowing action, only to sow on the Baking Field.
The Baking Field is not considered to be adjacent to any farmyard space or other card, regardless of its location, and does not benefit from the Planter Box.

card_text: When you play this card, immediately buy up to 2 animals from the general supply. Pay any 2 building resources for each sheep, any 3 for each wild boar, and any 4 for each cattle.
card_rulings: The animals can be the same or different.
Building resources are wood, clay, reed, and stone.
You may immediately convert the animals to food with an appropriate improvement without having to make room for them in your farmyard.
In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

card_text: Whenever you use a bread baking action, you can use the Beehive Oven to convert up to 1 grain into 4 food. Whenever you bake 1 or more grain into bread without using the Beehive Oven, you receive 1 additional food. When you play this card, you can also bake bread immediately.
card_rulings: Is both an oven and a baking improvement.
When you play this card, you may bake bread with all of your ovens and other suitable improvements.
Bread is not baked if schnaps is distilled or beer is brewed, or when a Water Mill, Windmill or Hand Mill is used.

card_text: Whenever you grow your family, you may pay 1 stone for 1 bonus point. When you play Birthstone, you may immediately do this for any new family member that you already received this round.
card_rulings: If you receive 2 new family members at once with Mother of Twins, you may pay 2 stone for 2 bonus points.

card_text: If you harvest at least 2 resources and breed at least 1 animal during a harvest, place 1 food from the general supply on this card. You may use this food at any time. At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each food on this card.
card_rulings: Food on this card is part of your personal supply, and counts for, e.g., Pilgrim.
Is activated by Almanac.
If you also have Harvest Helper, grain harvested from another player's field counts for this card.
At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each food on this card, up to a maximum of 3 bonus points.

card_text: Place 1 food on each remaining major improvement on the major improvements board. When these improvements are acquired, you receive the food.
card_rulings: If there are two major improvements on top of each other, place only 1 food on the revealed card.

card_text: Whenever you plow 1 or more fields, you receive 1 wood from the general supply for each field you just plowed.
card_rulings: Is not activated when you place a field without plowing, e.g., using the "Slash and Burn" special action, Cattle Collar, or Peat Burn-off.

card_text: You may keep up to 2 animals of the same type on each unsowed field orthogonally adjacent to your home. Different corrals may hold different types of animals. At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each of these fields that holds at least 1 animal.
card_rulings: The fields do not count as pastures.

card_text: You may keep up to 2 cattle on each unused space on your farmyard, to a maximum of 4 cattle.
card_rulings: The spaces still count as unused.

card_text: Whenever you have 2/3 different types of animals at the beginning of the field phase, you may add 1 more resource on 1/2 of your planted fields (or planted cards).
card_rulings: Animal cards like House Goat do not count, but animals kept on cards do.

card_text: When you play this card, immediately take the "Bake bread" a bread baking action.
card_rulings: Is an oven, but not a baking improvement.

card_text: When you play this card, immediately build a major improvement.
card_rulings: Pay the costs for the improvement normally.

card_text: When you play this card, you receive 1 vegetable.
Whenever another player sows, you may pay 1 food to the supply to immediately take a "Sow" action.
Pay 1 less wood/clay/stone for each wood/clay/stone room. Each round you build stables, pay 1 less wood for up to 2 stables.
Whenever you harvest at least 1 grain, you may immediately afterward exchange 1 grain from your personal supply for 1 vegetable.
When you play this card, immediately take a "Fences" action. Pay 1 wood to every other player who has at least 1 fence.
card_rulings: ERRATA: error in printing of this card, the following font small text is missing: "After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand".

card_text: When you play this card, each player may immediately move any amount of their planted resources to their personal supply.
card_rulings: This does not count as a harvest.

card_text: At the end of the game, you receive 1/2 bonus points for 3/4 stables, 1 bonus point if you have 3 stables, or 2 bonus points if you have 4 stables.
card_rulings: A stable used by Emergency Housing counts as a stable for the Hayloft.

card_text: Each round, you may place 1 guest token as you would a family member. If you do this, you must place 1 fewer person next round. You can not use this ability in consecutive rounds nor in round 14.
card_rulings: You can decide to use the Hired Helpers at the start of any of your turns, and at the latest when it's your next turn after the one in which you placed your last family member (including the occupant of the Reed Hut and other guests except for the one from the Keg).

You have to place the guest token in the same turn that you receive it, unless you skip that turn (e.g., because of the Robber or because of a special action). If another player has the Robber, Hired Helpers does not count as a erson left to place, unless you skipped a turn.

If you have a card that is activated by placing your last person or taking your last action, e.g., Magician or Opportunist, and you use that card when you place your last family member, you can no longer use the Hired Helpers that turn. You may choose not to use that card when you place your last family member, but then you have to use the Hired Helpers next turn (and it counts as a person left to place until then).

card_text: When you play this card, immediately either discard 1 begging card or take 1 animal (of your choice) from the supply.
card_rulings: If you take an animal, you may immediately convert it to food with an appropriate improvement without having to make room for it in your farmyard.
In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

card_text: Whenever you sow, each field you sow that is orthogonally adjacent to exactly 1 stable gets an additional 1 grain or 1 vegetable. Each field that is orthogonally adjacent to 2 or more stables, gets an additional 2 grain or 2 vegetables.
card_rulings: It does not matter if the stable is fenced.
You can only put extra grain on grain fields, and extra vegetables on vegetable fields.
Cards such as Acreage and Beanfield do not benefit from the Horse Barn.
If you also have the Winter Garden, grain or vegetable fields inside your home do not benefit from the Horse Barn.

card_text: Whenever you plow a field that is orthogonally adjacent to a planted field, you may take 1 grain or vegetable from that field and sow it immediately on the newly plowed field. Place 1 fewer grain or vegetable from the supply on the newly planted field.
card_rulings: If you use the "Plow Field and Sow" action space, you may sow before you plow.
If you plow more than 1 field at the same time (e.g., with a plow), you may only take grain from previously existing fields that contained grain before you started plowing.
You get a single sowing action that you can only use for one grain or vegetable per plowed field. This activates, e.g., the Smallholder and the Liquid Manure, but not the Scarecrow.
Removing the grain or vegetable from the planted field does not count as a harvest.

card_text: When you play this card, immediately take a "Sow" action.
card_rulings: In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

card_text: When you play this card, you receive 2 vegetables.
card_rulings: In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

card_text: Whenever you bake 1 or more grain into bread, you may pay 1 additional grain and 1 animal for 3 Bonus points. When you play this card, you may also take the "Bake bread" action.
card_rulings: Is activated when you use a baking improvement (with the "baking" symbol) to convert grain to food.
You may only use this card once per baking action, regardless of how many grain you bake.
Bread is not baked if schnaps is distilled or beer is brewed, or when a Water Mill, Windmill, or Hand Mill is used.
You may pay 1 additional grain and 1 animal for 2 Bonus points (up to a maximum of 6 bonus points.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Day Labourer" action space, you may immediately afterward pay 1 food to plow 1 field.
card_rulings: You can use the food from "Day Labourer" to pay for the field.

card_text: You may not use the Paddocks to fence unfenced farmyard spaces.
card_rulings: This is a fence building action, and activates Farmer, Hedge Keeper, and Stablehand.
In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

card_text: For each wild boar, cattle, or 2 sheep you convert into food, you may place 1 reed from the general supply on this card. You may use this reed to extend or renovate your home.
card_rulings: If you convert a single sheep into food, you don't receive any reed, not even if you already converted a single sheep earlier.
Reed on this card is not part of your personal supply. It does not count for Basketmaker's Workshop or Storehouse Clerk.
You may not use the reed on this card to pay for the Wooden Hut Extension, Clay Hut Extension, or Stone House Extension.
If you also have the Pelts, Tanner, Basin Maker, Taxidermist, and/or Brush Maker, you may use those cards as well as the Pelt Roof for each converted animal.

card_text: At the start of each round, you may place 1 wood on this card if you have zero wood in your supply. When this card has 5 wood, move the wood from this card to your personal supply.
card_rulings: You may not give wood to the general supply for nothing, unless you also have the Pilgrim.
If you receive wood at the start of the round, e.g., because of Rancher or Private Forest, you may use the Pine Forest before you receive that wood.
You must take the wood as soon as there are 5 wood on the card; you may not wait until there is 6 or more wood on the card.

card_text: When you play this card, you receive 1 Food for each planted plowed field in your farmyard. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: You do not receive any food for improvements with planted resources, such as Beanfield and Forester.
You receive only 1 food for a field with 2 stacks of grain because of the Scarecrow.
In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

card_text: Whenever you convert 1 clay into food using the Pottery A8 or Potter E214, you receive 1 additional food. The Pottery is now a minor improvement for you.
card_rulings: The Pottery still counts as major improvement as well, e.g., for Nosebag or Administration.

card_text: Once per round, you may exchange 2 food for a building resource of your choice.
card_rulings: Building resources are wood, clay, reed, and stone.

card_text: This card is an additional action space for all players. Any player who uses the Private School must pay you 1 food to play an occupation. All players must still pay any additional costs for their occupation (for Chief E172, Lover K291, etc).
card_rulings: If you use the Private School yourself, you do not need to have or to pay any food.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Day Labourer" action space you may immediately afterward build one room.
card_rulings: Pay costs for the room normally. You can use discounts from, e.g., Axe and Thatcher.
If you receive building resources for the "Day Labourer" action, e.g., because of Quarry or Clay Pit, you can use these to pay for the room.
Activates Wet Nurse, Building Tycoon, and Unusual Architecture.
If you also have the Oldest Occupation, you may first use the Pulley to build a new room and then the Oldest Occupation to grow a family member there.

card_text: Whenever you bake 1 or more grain into bread, you may pay 1 additional grain and 1 vegetable for 3 bonus points. When you play this card, you can also take the "Bake bread" action.
card_rulings: Is activated when you use a baking improvement (with the "baking" symbol) to convert grain to food.
