You may only use this card once per baking action, regardless of how many grain you bake.
Bread is not baked if schnaps is distilled or beer is brewed, or when a Water Mill, Windmill, or Hand Mill is used.
You may pay 1 additional vegetable for 2 bonus points (up to a maximum of 6 bonus points).

card_text: Whenever you use the "Plow 1 Field" action space, you receive 1 reed.
In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

When you play this card, immediately return a stable from your farmyard to your supply and extend your Wooden hut by 1 room. (The room does not cost anything, but you must pay the cost shown on this card.) After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: You can not use discounts on room building costs, e.g., from Ladder and Thatcher, when paying for this card.
You cannot remove a stable that is used for Emergency Housing.
In a solo game, this card is removed from the game after you play it.

card_text: Whenever you convert animals into food, you receive 1 additional food for each animal.
You may sow fields even if they already contain resources. Place the new resources on top of the existing ones. You cannot sow different resources on a single field.
card_rulings: If you receive extra resources when sowing, e.g., because of Smallholder or Liquid Manure, you also receive them when you use the Scythe.
Is activated by any sowing action, including those from Hobby Farmer and Five-year Plan, except sowing actions on empty fields only (e.g., Corn Storehouse).
You may also use the Scythe on improvements like the Acreage, Beanfield, and Copse, and on the indoors field of Winter Garden, and on the farmyard spaces used by No-Till Farming.
If you sow an empty field, you cannot use the Scythe on that same field in the same sowing action.
You cannot use the Scythe twice on the same field in the same sowing action.

card_text: Whenever you sow during an even-numbered round, add 1 additional resource of the appropriate type to 1 field you just sowed.
card_rulings: You may choose to sow an extra resource on cards like Forester and Beanfield.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Plow 1 Field" action space, you may immediately afterward sow 1 grain or 1 vegetable.
card_rulings: You get a single sowing action that you can only use for one grain or vegetable from your personal supply. This activates, e.g., the Smallholder and the Liquid Manure, but not the Scarecrow.

card_text: Areas of one or more unused farmyard spaces that are completely surrounded by fences or rooms are now automatically pastures. You may not use them any longer for any other purpose.
card_rulings: If you want, you can add fences of another colour where these pastures border your home, for clarity.

If a farmyard space is entirely surrounded by your home, it becomes a pasture as well, even though it is not connected to your other pastures. You may not create an unconnected pasture in any other way with this card.

The sides of your home do not count as fences (even when they help surround one of your pastures). If you create a pasture by placing a new room, this does not count as building fences, e.g. for Hedge Keeper. It does count as fencing a new pasture for Shepherd's Crook and Animal Breeder.
If Shared Wall is discarded with the Madonna Statue, incompletely fenced spaces are no longer pastures. Leave the fences on your farmyard; they have no effect.

card_text: At the beginning of each harvest, you may exchange 1 grain or vegetable for 1 sheep. Alternatively, you may exchange 1 sheep for 1 grain or vegetable.
card_rulings: Is activated before the field phase of the harvest.
If you take a sheep, you may immediately convert it to food with an appropriate improvement without having to make room for them in your farmyard.

card_text: You may keep up to 2 sheep on each unused space in your farmyard. You may not keep any animal other than sheep in your home. The Sheepdog must be fed 1 food each harvest.
card_rulings: The farmyard spaces where you keep the sheep still count as unused.
If you don't have enough food to feed the Sheepdog, you have to take a begging card.
The food for the dog must be paid during the feeding phase of the harvest.
If you already have played the House Goat, you cannot play the Sheepdog, and vice versa.
The Sheepdog does not count as an animal, e.g., for prerequisites of minor improvements.

card_text: At the end of the game, if you own a Fireplace, you receive 2 bonus points.
card_rulings: A Simple Fireplace counts as a fireplace; a Cooking Hearth doesn't.
You only receive the bonus once, even if you have 2 fireplaces.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Day Labourer" action space, you may immediately afterward take the "Sow" action.
card_rulings: If you receive resources on the "Day Labourer" action space, e.g., because of Seasonal Worker or Undergardener, you may use these in the sowing action.

card_text: At the end of the game, you receive 3 bonus points if you have at least 5 plowed fields, 8 grain and 4 vegetables.
card_rulings: Planted grain and vegetables count.
Improvements such as Acreage and Beanfield do not count as fields for this card.
At the end of the game, you receive 2 bonus points if you have at least 5 plowed fields, 8 grain and 4 vegetables.

card_text: Whenever another player builds a room or renovates, you may immediately perform this action yourself (without placing a family member). Pay the costs using 1 less building resource of your choice.
card_rulings: You cannot build a room when another player renovates, or vice versa.
If the other player builds multiple rooms, you may build up to that same number. The Stone Crane gives a discount on all of those rooms.
You can use cards like Axe or Renovator to reduce the costs of the new room(s) or the renovation.
Is also activated when another player builds a room or renovates because of an improvement or occupation, such as Wooden Hut Extension, Master Builder, or Builder's Trowel.
If the other player does an additional action, e.g., builds a stable as well as a room, or plays an improvement as well as renovates, you cannot do that action as well.

card_text: At any time, you may convert goods to food as follows: vegetables -> 4 food; sheep -> 2 food; wild boar -> 3 food; cattle -> 4 food. Whenever you use the "Bake bread" action, you may convert: grain -> 3 food.
card_rulings: With this card, you can convert several animals and vegetables to food at the same time. When you bake bread you can convert as many grain as you want. You may bake bread and convert other goods to food at the same time, in order to activate the Gypsy's Crock.
Is a cooking improvement and a baking improvement, but not an oven.
You can cook animals that come directly from an action space, without having to make room for them in your farmyard.

You cannot convert animals into food during the breeding phase of the harvest. After the breeding phase of the last harvest, the game ends immediately; you can not convert the animals received in the last harvest to food at all.

card_text: In each harvest, you can use the Stonecutter's Hall to convert at most exactly 1 stone to 3 food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 bonus points for 2/4/5 stone.
Whenever another player takes the "Fishing" action, you can take 1 grain or vegetable from each of your planted fields and place it in your personal supply.
card_rulings: This also applies to field cards such as Acreage and Beanfield.
You cannot take other resources, such as wood from Copse or Forester.
Using the Fish Farm does not count as harvesting.
Activates Sower when used, but not, e.g., Corn Storehouse or Gardener.

card_text: Once you no longer live in a Wooden Hut, one of your rooms can hold an additional Family member.
card_rulings: You can take a normal family growth action even if you have the same number of family members as rooms.
Cost 3W 1R. Requires 3 Occupations.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Take 1 Vegetable" action space, you receive 1 additional vegetable.

You may sow 1 of your rooms as if it were 1 field. Whenever you have an animal in your home (including House Goat K120), move all resources planted in this room to the general supply. Harvest resources from your room as normal and additionally at the end of rounds 6, 8, 10, and 12.
card_rulings: The Sheepdog does not count as an animal for this card.
You are allowed to sow in a room when you have an animal in your home, e.g. to use the Serf, but you lose the grain or vegetables immediately.
You cannot use the Scarecrow when you sow in a room.
The field in your home does not count as a field for prerequisites of minor improvements or in scoring at the end of the game.

card_text: Whenever you use the "1 Reed" action space, you receive 1 additional food. Whenever you use the "Day Labourer" action space, you receive 1 additional Reed.
At the end of the feeding phase of each Harvest, you may discard 1 Begging card that you received in any Harvest because you paid 1 Food less to feed your family.
card_rulings: You may not use this to discard Begging cards that you have received in other ways.

card_text: You may place newborn animals of any type on this card immediately after their birth. They cannot be used for any purpose or moved of this card, but are counted at the end of the game. (This card does not count as a pasture or stable.)
card_rulings: This card counts as available space for breeding animals.
Animals on this card are counted in bonus point calculations at the end of the game (such as that of Milking Stool, Milking Hand, Loom, etc.)

card_text: Place 1 Wood from the general supply on an empty field. This field still counts as a field, but can no longer be used for sowing or other uses. In the field phase of each Harvest, you receive 1 Food.
card_rulings: The field is no longer empty.
The wood may never be harvested from the field.

card_text: Immediately play an Occupation if you have fewer Occupations than each other player. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: You still must pay any additional costs for the occupation.

card_text: Whenever you extend your home, you may place 1 Wood from your supply on each of the new rooms. You cannot use this wood any more, but each is worth 1 Bonus point at the end of the game.
card_rulings: If you remove a room with a Wood on it (for example, with Downsizing), you also lose the Bonus point that Wood represents.

card_text: Immediately place a Family member that is not already placed on an Action space on the "After Family Growth also 1 Minor Improvement" Action space even if it is occupied by you or another player and take that action. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: In a 5-player game, you must wait for the "After Family Growth also 1 Minor Improvement" round card.
You must have another family member available as you play Back Door.

card_text: Once this game, at any time, you may place 1 Stone from the general supply on 1 Unused farmyard space next to your home. (It now counts as used.) If you do this, place 1 Food on each of the next 3 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food. (Backyard Well counts as a Well.)
card_rulings: Does not trigger the Flagon.
Does not activate Water Carrier.
Well Builder cannot be used to reduce its cost.
May be upgraded to Village Well, in which case Backyard Wel is discarded. The Stone is also removed from your farm and the space no longer counts as used.

card_text: Each Harvest, you may place 1 Food on this card from your supply. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for each food on this card. You may not hold Sheep, Wild Boar, or Cattle in your home. (Even if another card allows this).
card_rulings: This card can be used in conjunction with House Goat.
If you have the Birdcage, the Animal Tamer has no effect.
Does not apply to the last Harvest.

card_text: Place 1 Wood from the general supply on 1 Unused farmyard space. As long as the Wood remains, the space counts as used. You may return it at any time to convert animals to Food for the rest of the round. The conversion rates are: sheep to 2 Food; Wild boar to 3 Food; Cattle to 4 Food.
At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3/5 Bonus points for having at least 2/3/4/5 fields that are orthogonally adjacent to exactly one other field.

During the Feeding phase of each Harvest, you can use the Brazier to convert exactly 1 animal or Vegetable to Food as follows: Vegetable to 3 Food; Sheep to 2 Food; Wild boar to 3 Food; Cattle to 4 Food.
Whenever you use the "Plow 1 field" or "Plow 1 field and/or Sow" Action space, you also receive 1 Stone.

When you sow, you may sow on this card as if it were 1 field. Whenever you harvest goods from this card during the Field phase of a Harvest, you must exchange these harvested goods for 1 Bonus point each.
card_rulings: Goods that are harvested from this card at other times may not be converted to bonus points.
The bonus points should be recorded on your score sheet. Therefore, if you remove Cash Crop (e.g., with Madonna Statue) you still receive the bonus points.

card_text: Immediately return 1 or 2 stables from your farmyard to your supply. Afterward, Plow 1 field for every stable returned (in the same or different space). (Normal plowing rules still apply.) After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
Clockwork Man counts as 1 Occupation and 1 Minor Improvement.
card_rulings: Playing this card gives you a Bonus point for the Tutor.
Does not activate cards that trigger upon playing an Occupation, such as Patron, Bookshelf, Educator, etc.

card_text: For each Grain that you Bake, you may place 1 Food from your supply in 1 of your rooms. You may have a maximum of 1 Food in each room. You cannot use these Food any more, but each is worth 1 Bonus point at the end of the game.
card_rulings: Different cards (such as Pelts or Tapestry Weaver) may put food in the rooms. The 1 Food maximum is for each such card.
If you remove a room with a Food on it (for example, with Downsizing), you also lose the Bonus point that Food represents.

card_text: At the end of any Work phase in which you have taken a "Family Growth" action, you may take 2 Food from the general supply or pay 2 Food for 1 Bonus point.
card_rulings: Can only be activate once per round, even if you took a Family Growth action more than once.
The bonus points should be recorded on your score sheet. Therefore, if you remove the Crib (e.g. with Madonna Statue) you still receive the bonus points.

card_text: Place 1 Stone each on the next 2 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Stone.

From now on, one of your pastures can not contain animals. (Mark this pasture with 1 Clay from the general supply.) Whenever you use the "Plow 1 field" or "Plow 1 field and/or Sow" Action space, you may Plow 1 additional field.
card_rulings: May be combined with other plows or the Harrow I68.
The Donkey does not count as an animal, e.g. for prerequisites of minor improvements.
The pasture containing the Donkey may be changed. If this happens, move the Clay to indicate the new pasture that is being used.
The pasture containing the Donkey gives Animal Farmer 1 Bonus point, as the pasture cannot contain more animals than it currently does.
The Donkey can only live in a pasture on your farmyard, not in, for example, Forest Pasture, Wildlife Reserve, Animal Yard, a field (using Corral), etc.

card_text: Once during the game, when you build at least 1 fence, you may return 1 Wooden room that you built this game and build up to 7 additional fences from your supply at no cost. If there was a person in that room, move them into the rest of your home.
card_rulings: If you have Hill Farmer, count the number of rooms that you have after removing one.
If you have Pastor, it will still trigger upon downsizing if you are now the only player with 2 rooms, while the others have 3 or more.
The "After Renovation also fences" action cannot be combined with Downsizing when you renovate from a Wooden hut, as the renovation occurs first.

card_text: Add 4 to the current round and place 1 field on the corresponding Round space. At the start of that round, you may pay 1 Food to add the field to your farm.
Whenever you harvest the last good from a field and place it in your supply, you receive 1 Food.
card_rulings: You receive 1 Food when you harvest the last wood from Forester or Copse.

card_text: Whenever you harvest at least 2 goods during the Field phase of a Harvest, you may immediately afterward pay 2 Food to play an Occupation.
After the Field phase of a Harvest, you can use the Festival Hall to convert 1 Grain to 1 Building resource of your choice and 2 Food.
Whenever you use an Action space orthogonally adjacent to the "Fishing" Action space, you may immediately take 1 Food from the "Fishing" Action space if there is a Food on that space.
card_rulings: Does not trigger any improvements or occupations that Use the "Fishing" Action space.

card_text: Place 1 Clay from the general supply on 1 Unused farmyard space. It now counts as used. Whenever you use the "Fishing" Action space, you may place some or all of the Food on this card. At the end of the game, you receive -1/1/2/3/4 Bonus points for 0/1/4/6/8 Food on this card.
card_rulings: You may only place Food from the Action space itself, not food received due to Fisherman, Canoe, etc.

card_text: If you play this in Round 6 or before, place 1 Vegetable on the space for Round 8. At the start of this round, you receive the Vegetable.

At the start of each round, if you have at least 1 Grain field, place 1 Food from the general supply on this card. You may use this Food at any time. At the end of the game you receive 1/2 Bonus Points for 3/6 Food on this card.
Your pastures with stables may hold 2 different animal types. (This card does not change the capacity of your pastures.)
In the Feeding phase of the next Harvest, each of your family members (including newborns) require 1 Food less.

Each harvest you may convert up to 1 Clay into 3 Food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 Bonus points for 2/4/5 Clay. (A repurchased Pottery does not give you additional Bonus points, but can be used to convert an additional Clay each Harvest.)
card_rulings: After this card has been played, it counts both as a minor and as a major improvement, e.g., for the requirements of other cards.

card_text: When you play this card, choose one category from Grain, Vegetables, Sheep, Wild boar, Cattle, fields, pastures, or fences stables. At the end of the game, all players with the most Points in that category receive 2 Bonus points.
card_rulings: Forest Pasture does not count as a pasture for scoring, and thus does not add to your total when counting Points in the pastures category.

The Bonus points are awarded based on Points in the chosen category, not based on number of that item. Further, do not count Bonus points (for example, from Brewery or Schnaps Distillery) when counting points for a particular category.

card_text: At the start of each round, add 1 Grain from the general supply to this card, unless it has Grain equal to the number of players. Whenever you use the "Take 1 Grain" action, you also take all the Grain on this card. Whenever another player uses the "Take 1 Grain" action, return all Grain on this card to the general supply.
Whenever you renovate your home, or if you renovated this round, place 1 Food on the next 3 remaining Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.
In Round 14, you place 2 fewer Family members.
card_rulings: At the end of all of your turns in Round 14, you must have at least 2 family members left in your home. When you can no longer take actions and still have this be true, then you cannot take any more actions.

card_text: Once during the game, when you use the "Plow 1 field" Action space, you may Plow 2 fields instead and take a "Sow" action afterward.
You can use the "Build room(s) and/or Build stable(s)" Action space, even if another player has already placed a person on it.
You may build Major Improvement Fireplace and Cooking Hearths with a "Minor Improvement" action. Pay 1 Clay less for each.
card_rulings: Does not reduce the cost of the Simple Fireplace.

card_text: Whenever you play an Occupation, you receive 1 Food before you pay the cost of the Occupation.
Each Harvest, you may use the Mountain Cave to exchange up to 1 Wood for 1 Stone.

When you play this card, place 1 Food from the general supply on it. During the Field phase of each Harvest, add 1 Food from the general supply to this card if it has Food on it. At any time, you may take all of the Food from this card.
At the start of the Feeding phase of each Harvest, you may return 1 Sheep to the general supply to receive 1 Bonus point.
card_rulings: The bonus points should be recorded on your score sheet. Therefore, if you remove the Open Gate (e.g., with Madonna Statue) you still receive the bonus points.

card_text: At the end of the game, if you have all the animal types in your farm, you receive 1 Bonus point if you have at least 2 Sheep and 1 Bonus point if you have at least 2 Wild boar.

Immediately return 1 empty field to the general supply to fence a single farmyard space (in the same or different space.) (Normal fencing rules still apply.) You do not need to pay Wood for the fences. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: This is a "fences" action.

card_text: Place 1 Food on each of the next Round spaces, up to the number of planted fields in your farmyard. At the start of those rounds, you receive the food.
You immediately receive 1 Building resource of your choice. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.

The Ram counts as a Sheep in your farm for scoring and breeding, but must be fed 1 Food in the Feeding phase of each Harvest or it is discarded. Your sheep breed at the end of Rounds 6, 8, 10, and 12.
card_rulings: Counts as a Sheep for the purposes of determining bonus points for cards that count animals at the end of the game, such as Loom, Animal Holder, Estate Manager, etc.
Does not count as a Sheep otherwise, for example for determining food gained in Harvests from Spindle, Loom, Butter Churn, Milking Shed, etc.

card_text: Place 1 Food each on up to 3 Unused farmyard spaces from the general supply. When the space is used, you receive the Food.
card_rulings: May be combined with other cards that place Food on unused spaces, e.g., Butterfly Catcher and Pine Cones.

card_text: You may Sow and harvest Reed in your fields as if it were Grain. At the end of the game, you may exchange 1/4/6/8 Reed from your supply or from your fields for 1/2/3/4 Bonus points.
card_rulings: Does not interact with anything that specifies Grain (e.g. Permaculturist, Planter Box, etc.). But it does interact with cards that mention sown "Resources" or "Goods" (e.g., Fieldsman).

card_text: Whenever you use the "Plow 1 field" Action space, you may Plow 2 fields if they are not diagonally or orthogonally adjacent to each other instead of 1.
Place 4 Stone on this card from the general supply. In the Field phase of each Harvest, you may move 1 Stone from this card to your supply.
At the end of any turn where you placed a person on a "Renovation" Action space, you may return that person to your farm. (That person takes another action later in the round.)
At any time, you can convert pairs of goods to Food as follows: Grain and Vegetable -> 5 Food; Grain and Sheep -> 4 Food; Grain and Wild boar -> 5 Food; Grain and Cattle -> 6 Food.
card_rulings: If you use the Steamer to convert Grain and animals to food, this counts as converting animals to food. (For example, for Pelts, Slaughterman, etc.)

card_text: Once during the game, when you use either the "Plow 1 field" or "Plow 1 field and/or Sow" Action space, you can Plow up to 4 fields instead of 1.

Remove 1 built stable and place is on this card (you may not build it again later). Whenever you play a Minor Improvement, take 1 Building resource of your choice from the general supply before paying the costs of the Improvement.
card_rulings: The stable still counts as a built stable, but is no longer considered to be on your farmyard.
You may receive resources from the Storage Shed when you play Major Improvements as Minor Improvements.

card_text: Place 1 Food on each of the next Round spaces, up to the number of completed rounds. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.
Once per turn, when paying a cost, you may discard 1 card from your hand to pay 1 less Building resource of your choice.
card_rulings: The discarded card is discarded face down.
May only be used to pay costs during your turn.
Once per turn, when paying a cost, you may discard 1 card from your hand to pay 1 less Building resource of your choice except stone.

card_text: Immediately add 1 animal of the appropriate type to each Accumulation space containing 2 or more animals. If you have space on your farm, you may take 1 of the added animals. After you play this card, pass it to the player on your left, who adds it to his/her hand.

When you play this card, you receive 1 Vegetable. Once each round, you may move 1 Vegetable from your supply to a future Round space. If it is at least 5 rounds away, add 1 Vegetable from the general supply. At the start of that round, you must convert each of those Vegetables to 2 Food or 1 Bonus point.
card_rulings: The Vegetable received initially will trigger Market Woman. Any Vegetables placed on future Round spaces do not trigger Market Woman when you convert them to Food or Bonus points.
The bonus points should be recorded on your score sheet. Therefore, if you remove the Wine Closet (e.g., with Madonna Statue) you still receive the bonus points.
Up to a maximum of 5 bonus points.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Plow 1 field" Action space, add 2 Food from the general supply to this card. You receive the Food on this card when you use the "Fishing" action. Each Harvest, return 1 Food from this card (if any) to the general supply.
Immediately remove 1 empty field from your farmyard and receive 4 Wood. (That space now counts as unused).
card_rulings: You may not remove a field which causes other fields to be isolated.

card_text: Place 1 Food on the next 5 remaining Round spaces. At the start of these rounds' Work phase, place the Food on an Action space of your choice. (The next player to use this space receives the Food.)
Place 1 Food each on the next 2/3/4/5 remaining Round spaces if you have 1/2/3/4 pastures. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.
You receive 2 Bonus points for either Grain or Vegetables missing from your farm at the end of the game. (The fruit replaces this type of good.) (You still lose the points for the missing good.)
card_rulings: If you are missing both types of crops, you still only get 2 bonus points.
The score for the missing type of crop still counts as a negative for the Constable and Yeoman Farmer.
Apple Garden does not count as a crop for the prerequisites of minor improvements.

card_text: In the Returning home phase of each round, you may harvest 1 field.
card_rulings: You may harvest goods which are sown onto occupations or improvements.
The returning home phase occurs after the work phase but before the end of the round, and before harvests.

card_text: Immediately place a marker on an Action space of your choice. At the start of each round, you must move it to an orthogonally adjacent revealed Action space. Any player that uses that space also receives 1 Food.
card_rulings: You may take the Badger food before using an action space.
Chamberlain reveals action spaces.
May not be moved to "additional" action spaces (occupations or improvements).
You cannot place the Badger on the spot you used to play it and immediately take 1 Food.

card_text: During each Harvest, you may pay 1 Wood to Bake bread at the start of the Feeding phase if you have an Improvement with the bread symbol.
For every Harvest remaining when you play this card, you receive 1 Bonus point.
card_rulings: If you play this card at the start of (or during) a harvest, that harvest is not counted.

card_text: Each time you convert a Grain or Vegetable into Food, you may place it on this card. For each field you Sow, you may take 1 Good of the same type from this card and add it to that field. (Goods on this card do not count for points when scoring.)
card_rulings: Plowed field (so Acreage and Beanfield do not combo).

card_text: In each Feeding phase, you receive 1 Food.
At the start of each round, if you have no Clay in your supply, place 1 Clay on this card. At any time when this card has 4 or more Clay on it, you may move all of the Clay to your personal supply.
card_rulings: You may not return clay to the general supply for no reason.

card_text: Place 1 Food each on 3 Action spaces (unoccupied or occupied) from the general supply. The next player to use the Action space receives the Food.
card_rulings: Revealed action spaces only.

card_text: When you play this card, you receive 1 Wild boar. You may keep 1 Wild boar in each of your pastures that hold Sheep. (Pastures can still only hold the normal amount of animals).
card_rulings: A pasture that can hold two animals can only hold two animals, not two sheep and a boar.

card_text: Place 4 Food from the supply on this card. This food can only be used to pay for Occupations.
card_rulings: You may not use food from this card to pay for any extra occupation costs (e.g., Chief).

card_text: Place 1 Food on the next 5 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food. If you play an Occupation before receiving the Food, immediately remove this cards remaining Food from future Round spaces.
Place 1 Grain each on the spaces for rounds 7 and 9. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Grain.
Whenever you play an Occupation, you receive 1 Wood.
When you play this card, you may immediately fence 1 space in your farmyard. (You do not need to pay Wood for the fences).
card_rulings: This counts as "Building fences" but not a fence action.
You cannot build more than 15 fences using this card.
You must still follow fencing rules (connect to existing pastures).

card_text: If you have 1/2 plowed fields, immediately pay 1/2 Food to play 1/2 Occupations.
Whenever you play an Improvement, pay 1 Wood/Stone less if you chose Wood/Stone as the cost of this card. (Mark this card with 1 Wood/ Stone from the supply.)
When you play this card, immediately Renovate your Wooden hut to a Clay hut for free.
card_rulings: A planted card which has goods you harvest during the field phase counts (Forester, Copse).
A field with goods on it you do not harvest does not count (Apple Tree, Vineyard).

card_text: You no longer need Reed when you renovate your home.
The Well is a Minor Improvement for you. When you build the Well, place 2 Food on the next 5 Round spaces instead of 1 Food. (If you have already built the Well, then this card has no effect.)
card_rulings: Goblet does not work if you have already built Village Well.

card_text: Whenever you pay Food to play an Occupation or a Minor Improvement, you may pay up to 2 Food less.
card_rulings: You must still pay any additional food costs specified by the card.

card_text: You can't Bake bread with the Grain Mill Oven. Clay and Stone Ovens are Minor Improvements for you. You may discard this instead of paying an Oven's cost. When you play this card, you can immediately take the "Bake bread" action.
card_rulings: Grain Mill Oven is not an oven, it can not be upgraded to any oven upgrade (Baker's Kitchen, Baker's Oven).
Does not have a bake symbol so does not activate Charcoal Burner.
Can only bake when playing if you have another card which allows you to bake.

card_text: Place 3 Clay and 2 Stone from the supply on this card. At the end of each round, you may pay 2 Food to receive 1 of these resources. Once this card is empty, it provides room for 1 Family member.
card_rulings: Any extra family members in your house move into and occupy the Grotto when it is emptied.
If the Grotto is removed (or in any way turned down) then a peep living in it is moved into the rest of your house.
Grotto does not count as a room in your house.

card_text: Once during the game, when you use either the "Plow 1 field" or the "Plow 1 field and/or Sow" Action space, you can place 1 Wood from your supply on 1/2 orthogonally adjacent (revealed or unrevealed) Action spaces (to the used plow space) to Plow 1/2 additional fields.
card_rulings: Can be combined with other plows on the same action due to the word "additional".

card_text: One of your rooms can hold 1 additional Family member.
Each Work phase, the third person you place can use the "Plow 1 field" Action space even if it is occupied.
card_rulings: You may use the Hay Rake even if you occupy the space and/or multiple other players occupy the space.
If you have played the Acrobat and you use your third person to take "Travelling Players", you can then move to Plow 1 Field even if it is occupied.

card_text: Place 3 Food on each remaining Round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.
card_rulings: Horse does not count (only Sheep, Wild Boar and Cattle).

card_text: Immediately build a Fireplace for free or upgrade a Fireplace to a Cooking Hearth.
When you play this card, you may immediately play up to 2 Occupations. (You must still pay any additional costs of the Occupations.)
card_rulings: You do not need to pay food for those occupations (unless they have an additional cost).

card_text: Your home may hold 1 additional animal. You may keep more than 1 type of animal in your home.
card_rulings: If you have played the House Goat, you may not hold any animals in your home.

card_text: For you, all "Bake Bread" actions now read "Bake Bread or place 1 Food from your supply on Kitchen Table." At any time, you may return 1 Food from this card to your supply and take a "Bake Bread" Action.
card_rulings: The bread symbol on this card is an error.

card_text: The Lighthouse provides room for 1 Family member.
card_rulings: Any extra family members in your house move into and occupy the Lighthouse when it is emptied.

card_text: When you play this card, you may return 2 different animals to the general supply to Plow 2 fields.
card_rulings: The returned animals come from your farm.

card_text: Whenever at the end of a Work phase, you have at least 2 Family members occupying 2 orthogonally adjacent Action spaces, you receive 1 Food.
card_rulings: Action spaces do not need to be the same dimensions.

card_text: Place 1 Food on each of the next Round spaces up to the number of planted fields in your farmyard. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.
Add 4 and 7 to the current round and place 1 Wild boar on each corresponding Round space. At the start of these rounds, you can pay 1 Food to take the Wild boar.
card_rulings: The placed Wild boar comes from the general supply.

card_text: Place 3 Stone on the "Plow 1 field" Action space from the general supply. Any player who uses that space may also receive 1 Stone from it. If there is no Stone on that space, you may discard this card and Plow 1 field.
card_rulings: The stone may be bought with Stone Buyer.
If stone is placed on the "Plow 1 Field" space for other reasons (Mailman) then you may not discard this card to plow.

card_text: Every harvest you may convert 1 Reed into 4 Food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 Bonus points for having 1/3/4 Reed. You do not receive additional Bonus points from the Basket Maker's Workshop.
Whenever you build a Stone room, you can Plow up to 1 field. If you paid at least 1 Stone for that room, you may Plow up to 2 fields instead.
card_rulings: If you build a stone room using Stone House Extension, Mason, etc, you may plow one field.
If you build more than 1 stone room at the same time, you can plow more than 2 fields.

card_text: At the end of the game, you receive 4/2 Bonus points if your home has at most 2/3 rooms.
Place 1 Food on each remaining Round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.
You can Renovate your Clay hut to a Stone house at any time without using the "Renovate" action. (You must still pay for the Renovation.)
Whenever you add a room to your Clay hut, you can pay 2 Clay, 1 Grain and 1 Food instead of 5 Clay and 2 Reed.
card_rulings: Sets a new optional base cost for clay rooms.
Can be combined with Bricklayer, a clay room costs 1F 1G.

card_text: Place a marker on the next round. During that round, you place 1 fewer Family member. At the end of that round, remove the marker and play 2 Occupations for free.
card_rulings: You must still pay any additional costs of the occupation.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Fishing" Action space, you may also convert up to 1 Wood to 3 Food.
Whenever you use the "Bake Bread" Action, you may convert 1/2 Clay into 1/2 Stone.

Once during the game, when you use the "Plow 1 field" Action space, you may Plow all 3 middle spaces on your farmyard instead of Plowing 1 field. (The middle 3 spaces are those surrounded by exactly 8 other spaces.)
card_rulings: The three spaces are the three farmyard spaces that do not touch the edge of the farmyard in any way.
You may only use the effect of this card if all three spaces are able to be plowed.
You must plow all three spaces with this plow.

card_text: Place 1 building resource of each type on this card. These building resources can only be used to pay for Minor Improvements.
card_rulings: These resources are not "in your supply" and therefore cannot be used in combination with Storehouse Clerk or contribute to the Joinery/Pottery/Basketmaker's Workshop bonuses.
A major improvement played "as a minor" or that "is a minor to you" satisfies the prerequisite.

card_text: If you have the Joinery or Sawmill, you do not have to pay the Wood cost for this card. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for every 2 Improvements in front of you that have a printed Wood cost.
Whenever you use the "Fishing" Action space, you receive an additional 1 Food and 1 Wood.
Whenever you use the "Plow 1 field" Action space, you may immediately afterward take 1 Grain or 1 Vegetable from the general supply and Sow it.
card_rulings: You receive the Grain or Vegetable from the supply before sowing it (so therefore it triggers cards like Market Woman).

card_text: If you have an Oven, you can convert Vegetables into Food at any time. Take 4 Food for each Vegetable.
Immediately add 1 Grain from the supply to each of your planted Grain fields and 1 Vegetable to each of your planted Vegetable fields.
card_rulings: This card has no effect on fields with other resources planted on them.

card_text: You immediately receive 5 Food.
When you play this card, you may either pay 1 Wood for 1 Bonus point or receive 1 Wood and pass this card to the player on your left who adds it to his/her hand.
card_rulings: The wood comes from the supply.

card_text: Place this card on or beside your farm. If you place it beside your farm, you gain 2 extra farmyard spaces (to be scored as normal) and you receive 1 Bonus point. If you place it on your farm, cover two orthogonally adjacent unused spaces which no longer count as part of your farm.
card_rulings: If you extend your farm with this card, you do not receive any additional points for the Cloister Dweller.
If you cover part your farm with this card, Cloister Dweller scores as if those two spaces were not a part of your farm.

card_text: Whenever every other player has more Family members than you, you may immediately build 1 or more rooms by paying their cost.
card_rulings: You may activate the Wood Saw at any time, provided every other player has more family members than you.
This card has no effect in solo games.

card_text: When you play this card, take 1 Grain for every 2 completed rounds of the game.
Place 1 cattle, 1 grain, and 1 cattle on the next 3 round spaces (in that order). At the start of these rounds, you receive the good.
Your next 5 fences cost you nothing.
At the start of Rounds 8, 10, 12, and 14, you may pay 1 Wood to builds stable if you have more fields than stables.
Place 1 Food on each of the next Round spaces, up to the number of your played Occupations. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.
(This card has no effect.)
Add 3 to the current round and place a marker on the corresponding Round space. At the start of that round, you may play 1 Occupation for free.
You can use any "Family growth" Action space, even if it is occupied by another player.
Whenever you Bake 1 or more Grain into bread, you receive 1 additional Food if you have at least 1 Sheep and 1 additional Food if you have at least 1 Cattle.
During the Feeding phase of each Harvest, your newborns require 2 Food (instead of 1).
Whenever you build an Oven or a Cooking Hearth, you pay up to 2 building resources less of your choice.
Each Harvest, you may convert up to 1 Wood to 3 Food. (The Coke Stove cannot cook animals.)
When you play this card, you receive 4 Food. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND
During the Feeding phase of the Harvest, you receive 1 Grain/Vegetable if you chose Grain/Vegetable as the cost of this card. (Mark this card with 1 Grain/Vegetable from the supply.)
Whenever another player (or, in Solo game, you) uses the "3 Wood" Action space, you receive 1 Grain.
Whenever you Sow a field that is orthogonally adjacent to a pasture, you may place extra good from the supply on it.

Immediately take all the food from the "Fishing" accumulation space and place an equal amount of wood from the general supply on the space afterward. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND

Whenever you extend your home, you may place 1 Food from the supply on this card for each new room. During the Feeding phase of the Harvest, you receive 1 Food from the supply for each Food on this card.

Immediately place as much Food as you choose from your personal supply on this card. At the start of each round, you may discard 1 Food from this card for a Guest token which you place that round as you would a Family member.
Whenever you build a Major Improvement, you may play 1 Occupation for free.
At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/4 Bonus points for having exactly 4/3/2 Family members.
Whenever you receive Wild boar outside the Breeding phase of a Harvest, you may immediately convert them into 4 Food each.
Whenever you play another Improvement, you may pay 1 additional building resource of a type in the printed cost to receive 3 Food.

Place 1 Reed from the supply on 1 of your unused farmyard spaces. At the start of each round, you must place 1 Reed from the supply on another unused space orthogonally adjacent to a previously placed Reed (if able). These spaces count as used. (You may not remove the Reed or use the spaces.)

At the start of the returning home phases of rounds 3 and 6, you can move your person from the "Plow 1 field" action space to an unoccupied action space and take that action. If you do not occupy "Plow 1 field", you receive 1 food.
When you play this card, you can return up to 3 stables from your farmyard to your supply and receive 3 Wood for each.
During the Field phase of each Harvest you may harvest all the goods planted in 1 of your fields.
The edge of your farm now forms a natural border for pastures. (This does not count as building fences or taking a "Fences" action.)
Whenever you use the "Plow 1 field" Action space, you receive 2 Clay for each field you Plow.
Immediately take 1 building resource from 2 Accumulation spaces. The resources taken must be different types. AFTER YOU PLAY THIS CARD, PASS IT TO THE PLAYER ON YOUR LEFT, WHO ADDS IT TO HIS/HER HAND
Each Harvest, you may convert 1 Clay into 2 Food or 1 Bonus point.
From now on, one of your pastures containing a stable may not hold animals. Whenever you take Reed from an Action space, you receive 1 Bonus point.

Place 1 Sheep, 1 Wild boar and 1 Cattle on the next 3 Round spaces in this order. At the start of these rounds' Work phase, place the animal on an Action space of your choice. (The next player to use this space receives the animal.)
As long as you have a pasture orthogonally adjacent to your home, you may keep animals of any type on this card up to the number of rooms in your home.

At the end of each Work Phase, you may place 1 Food from your supply on Piggy Bank. You cannot use this Food anymore. At any time, you may discard 6 Food on Piggy Bank to build a Major Improvement for free.
At any time, you may convert 1 Reed into 1 Grain.
Place 3 Stone from the supply on this card. You may use this Stone only for renovations and only before the end of Round 11.

For each Vegetable you convert to Food, you may place 1 of the Food on this card. You may not have more Food on this card than your number of plowed fields. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for each Food on this card.
Place 1 Food on each remaining even-numbered Round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.
When you Sow, you can plant up to 3 Stone on this card. The Stone is treated the same as sown Vegetables and is harvested during the Field phase. (This card does not count as a Field when scoring.)

Whenever you use either the "Plow 1 field" or "Plow 1 field and/ or Sow" Action space, you can Plow 2 fields instead of 1. Whenever you Sow, place 1 fewer resource from the general supply on each field you Sow.
During the Feeding phase of each Harvest, you may use the Rum Distillery to convert at most 1 Grain into 3 Food and 1 Bonus point.

You can place exactly two people immediately after one another if at least one of them uses the "Sheep Market", "Pig Market", or "Cattle Market" accumulation space.
When you play this card, you immediately get 1 food. For each occupation you play, you get an additional "Bake Bread" action.
When you play this card, you immediately get 1 bonus point for each complete round left to play. You may no longer renovate your house.
For each person you have, you must pay an additional 1 clay and 1 food to play this card.
In the field phase of each harvest, if you have at least 1/4/7 sheep, you get 1/2/3 food. During scoring, you get 1 bonus point for every 3 sheep.
Place 1 food on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the food.
Each time you use the "Fishing" accumulation space, place 1 food on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
In the field phase of each harvest, you get 1 food for every 3 sheep and 1 food for every 2 cattle you have.
Each time you use one of the four action spaces above the "Fishing" accumulation space, you get 1 additional food.
At the start of each round, if you live in a wooden house, you get 1 food.
At the start of the field phase of each harvest, if you have at least 1 grain field, 1 vegetable field, and 1 empty field, you get 3 food.
Each time you use the "Grain Seeds" action space, if the "Farmland" action space is occupied, you also get 3 food.
Place 1 grain each on the remaining spaces for rounds 5, 8, 11 and 14. At the start of these rounds, you get the grain.
This card is a field that can only grow vegetables.
card_rulings: This card works differently in Revised Agricola than in the original game: it actually counts as a field when scoring.

card_text: Place 1 wood on each remaining even-numbered round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the wood.
Each time you use the "Day Laborer" action space, you also get 3 clay.
At any time, you can exchange 2/3/4 clay for 1/2/3 stone.
Place 1 wild boar on each of the next 2 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the wild boar.
Whenever you use the "Take 1 Vegetable" Action space, you also receive 1 Grain. When you play this card, you receive 1 Vegetable from the supply.
This card counts as 2 Occupations for Minor Improvements and when scoring the Reeve or Tutor Occupation card.
card_rulings: Playing the Academic counts as 1 occupation for the Bread Paddle and Bookshelf.

card_text: Whenever another player Bakes bread, you can Bake bread if you have a Baking Improvement with the baking symbol. If you take a Bake action yourself (not using this card), you receive 1 additional Food.
card_rulings: Is activated when another player uses a baking improvement (with the baking symbol) to convert grain to food.
Is not activated if another player uses the "Sow and Bake Bread" action space, but does not bake.
Bread is not baked if schnaps is distilled or beer is brewed, or when a Water Mill, Windmill, or Hand Mill is used.

card_text: During each Harvest, you may bake bread at the start of the Feeding phase if you have an Improvement with the baking symbol. When you play this card, you may bake bread as an additional action.
card_rulings: You may only use the Baker to bake bread just once each harvest, but you may use all of your ovens and other improvements to bake as much bread as you can.

card_text: Once during the game, at any time after your home reaches at least 5 rooms, you may extend it by 1 room at no cost.
card_rulings: You may place a room tile on this card to show that you have not yet taken the extension action.
You do not have to use an action space to build a room with the Master Builder.

card_text: Whenever you use a Family member's action to take Wood, you receive an additional 1 Food.
card_rulings: Is activated when you use an action space on which wood is placed each round. You cannot use such an action space just to use this card if the action space contains no wood (e.g., because of the Wood Distributor).
Is activated even if you leave all the wood on the action space because of Basket, Mushroom Collector, or Pig Catcher.
Is activated when you use an action space that contains wood because of the Wood Distributor.
Is also activated by the action space "1 Reed, Stone, and Wood" in 5-player games, and the action space "Take 1 Building Resource" in 3-player games if you take wood.
Is activated when you play Building Material.
Is not activated when you receive wood because of any other minor improvement or an occupation.

card_text: At the end of the game, you can discard up to 2 Begging cards without losing points for them.
In the Feeding phase of each Harvest, the Master Brewer can convert up to 1 Grain to 3 Food.
card_rulings: Brewing beer does not count as baking.
The grain that you want to convert into 3 food may not be on fields; it must be taken from your personal supply.

card_text: You receive 1 Food from the supply for each grain that is baked whenever any player (including you) bakes bread.
card_rulings: Bread is not baked if schnaps is distilled or beer is brewed, or when a Water Mill, Windmill or Hand Mill is used.
Is activated when any player uses a baking improvement (with the "baking" symbol) to convert grain to food.
Is not activated if anybody uses the "Sow and Bake Bread" action space, but does not bake.

card_text: Whenever you convert Wild boar to Food, you place the boar on this card. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 Bonus points for 2/3/4 slaughtered Wild boar.
card_rulings: A converted wild boar may either be placed on the Tanner, Taxidermist, or Brush Maker; you cannot use two of these cards for the same animal.
If you also have the Basin Maker and/or the Pelts, you may use those cards as well as the Brush Maker for each converted wild boar.
Wild boar markers on this card do not count in the scoring at the end of the game.

card_text: Pay 1 Reed less to build each room, for each Renovation, and for each of the Water Mill, Half-timbered House, Chicken Coop, Holiday House, Mansion and Corn Storehouse.
card_rulings: Can be used for every room you build, if you build more than 1 room.
If you also have other cards that reduce costs for building rooms, e.g., the Ladder, you may use all of these cards for the same action.
The Thatcher has no effect if the number of reeds required is already lowered to 0, e.g. by using both Clay Roof and Frame Builder when building a new room.

card_text: At any time, you can use the Turner to convert any number of Wood to 1 Food each.
You can use any Build room(s) or Family growth Action space, even if another player has already placed a person on it.
card_rulings: You may not use the same action space in the same round with 2 of your own people.

If the action space provides a choice of actions, you may choose either. For example, you may take the "Traveling Players" action on the "Build 1 Room or Traveling Players" action space in the 5-player game, or play an occupation on the "1 Occupation or Family Growth" action space.
It is not allowed to use an empty accumulating action such as "Traveling Players", not even if it activates other improvements or occupations.

card_text: The next time you build fences, you receive 1 Wild boar. Each time you build at least 1 fence after that, you receive 1 Cattle.
card_rulings: You only receive 1 new animal per turn, even if several pastures are created with the same action.
Is activated when you use the Fence Overseer, or when you play the Mini Pasture.
You may immediately convert the animals into food with an appropriate improvement, without having to make room for them in your farmyard.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Fishing" Action space, you can choose to take twice as much Food as is on the space. If you do this, you must give 1 Food each to the owners of the Fishing Rod, Raft, Canoe, Fish Trap, and Landing Net.
card_rulings: Only the food that is lying on the action space is doubled. Take the food from the action space, and the same amount from the general supply.
You only give food to another player if he has the improvement in front of him on the table.
If a player has played more than one of these improvements, you give him more than one food.
You do not have to pay anything for an item if you own it yourself.
It is possible that you have to pay more food than you receive.
If you won't have enough food to pay the owners of the listed improvements after using the Fisherman, you are not allowed to use it.
The effect of the Fisherman is optional. If you do not take the double amount of food, you do not have to pay anything to the other players.
Is not activated by using the Net Fisherman.

card_text: If you have an Oven, you can convert your animals into Food at any time: Take 2 food for each Sheep, 3 for each Wild boar and 4 for each Cattle.
card_rulings: The Meat-seller may not be used after upgrading an oven to a Baker's Kitchen or Bakehouse, unless you have another oven.
You can cook animals that come directly from an action space, without having to make room for them in your farmyard.

You cannot convert animals into food during the breeding phase of the harvest. After the breeding phase of the last harvest, the game ends immediately; you can not convert the animals received in the last harvest to food at all.
Ovens are not a cooking improvement, even if you have the Meat Seller. Using the Meat Seller does not activate the Schnitzel Mallet.

card_text: You can use the "Take 1 Vegetable", "Plow 1 Field" and "Plow Field and/or Sow" action spaces even if another player has already placed a person on the space.
card_rulings: You may not use the same action space in the same round with 2 of your own people.
You can use the Field Warden when you move a family member to the "Plow Field and/or Sow" space because of the Countryman.

card_text: Include the "2 Wood" Action card from the 3-player game as an additional forest. At the start of each round, place 2 Wood on the card. Any player who uses this action space must pay you 2 Food.
card_rulings: If you use the Master Forester yourself, you do not need to have or to pay any food.

The food must be paid before the wood is collected. If the player who wants the wood gets food for this action, for example because of the Berry Picker, he may not use this food to pay for the Master Forester.

card_text: At the end of the game, you only lose points for Unused spaces and Begging cards.
card_rulings: Write down the points you gain by using the Yeoman Farmer as bonus points. The number of bonus points you get is equal to the number of scoring categories for which you would otherwise have scored negative points.
Negative points that are compensated by the Yeoman Farmer are not counted as negative for the Constable.
If you use the Horse to compensate for a missing type of animals, you still receive a bonus point for that animal from the Yeoman Farmer (and 2 bonus points from the Horse).
The Yeoman Farmer also compensates negative (bonus) points for Forest Path M048, Built-in Oven M044, etc.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Day Laborer" Action space, you also receive 1 Vegetable.
Whenever you use the "Traveling Players" action on an Action space, you receive 1 Grain in addition to the Food.
card_rulings: If another player uses the Juggler with a "Traveling Players" action, he pays you 1 food.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Take 1 Grain" Action space, you also receive 1 Vegetable.
Whenever you use the "Traveling Players" action on an Action space, you can leave 1 Food on the space and receive 1 Vegetable instead.
card_rulings: If another player uses the Juggler with a "Traveling Players" action, he pays you 1 food.
If you have the Dancer as well, you receive at least 4 food and 1 vegetable when you leave one food on the action space.

card_text: At the end of a 3/4/5 player game, if no player has more animals of any type than you, you receive 2/3/4 Bonus points.
card_rulings: If a player has the Horse, it does not count as 1 animal of a missing type of his choice when counting animals for the Estate Manager.
The House Goat does not count as an animal.

card_text: At any time, you can use the Dock Worker to convert 3 Wood to either 1 Clay, 1 Reed or 1 Stone, or to convert 2 Clay, 2 Reed or 2 Stone to 1 other building resource.
card_rulings: Building resources are wood, clay, reed, and stone.

card_text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for each room in your Stone house. (In total, you receive 3 points per room instead of 2.) Playing this card costs an additional 2 Food.
card_rulings: The bonus points from Chief comes in addition to bonus points from cards such as Half-timbered House or Mansion.

card_text: If another player plays the Chief card, you can play this card immediately at no cost. (You can also play it using an Action space in the usual way.) At the end of the game, you receive 3 Bonus points if you have a Stone house, 1 if you have a Clay hut.
card_rulings: If you play the Chief yourself, you may not play the Chief's Daughter at the same time.
In a solo game, you can only play this card using an action space.
If you have the Clogs as well, you get points for both cards.
If you play the Chief's Daughter because the Chief is played, this activates the Bread Paddle, Bookshelf, Patron, and Educator, but not the Writing Desk, Perpetual Student, or Therapist.

card_text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for each Occupation that you play after this one.
card_rulings: You may note the bonus points on the scoring pad immediately, or you can simply lay out your occupations in the order that you play them.
The Academic counts as two occupations.
If you keep this card after playing a round in a series of solo games, you also receive bonus points for permanent occupation cards kept in play after subsequent games.
Up to a maximum of 6 bonus points.

card_text: Whenever you build at least 1 fence, you can build 3 additional fences without paying any additional wood. (You can only place fences if they enclose a complete pasture.)
card_rulings: Is activated when you use the Fence Overseer, Fence Builder or Fence Deliveryman, or when you play the Mini Pasture.
If you also have the Farmer, Stablehand, Wood Carver or Sawhorse, you may use all of those cards during the same fence building action.

The free fences from the Hedge Keeper are built in the same fence building action as all other fences, but in an order of your choosing. However, you may not use the Hedge Keeper for the first fence you build in a turn.
Can only be used once per turn.
Only after building the extra fences, you need to follow the conditions for building fences (i.e., no open pastures).
You may choose to build fewer than 3 fences, for example, if you do not have 3 unbuilt fences left.
If you build no free fences, or fewer than 3, you cannot save the other free fences for later rounds.

card_text: You receive 1 additional Wood whenever you use a Family member's action to take Wood.
card_rulings: Is activated when you use an action space on which wood is placed each round. You cannot use such an action space just to use this card if the action space contains no wood (e.g. because of the Wood Distributor).
Is activated even if you leave all the wood on the action space because of Basket, Mushroom Collector, or Pig Catcher.
Is activated when you use an action space that contains wood because of the Wood Distributor.
Is also activated by the action space "1 Reed, Stone, and Wood" in 5-player game, and the action space "Take 1 Building Resource" in 3-player game if you take wood.
Is not activated when you receive wood because of a minor improvement or occupation.

card_text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each room in your Wooden hut.
Play this card during Stage 1. At the start of Round 11, you can extend your hut by 1 room at no cost, as long as you have not yet renovated to a Stone house.
card_rulings: When the card is played, put a room tile on the round 11 action space as a reminder.
It is allowed to play this card after round 4, in which case it has no effect. It still counts as a played occupation, e.g. for the prerequisite of an improvement.
If you choose not to build a free room at the start of round 11, you cannot build it later in the game.

card_text: Whenever you use a "Minor Improvement" or "Minor or Major Improvement" action, you can pay 1 Food to use the action a second time.
card_rulings: If you use a "major or minor improvement" action, you can either play 2 major or 2 minor improvements, or 1 major and 1 minor improvement.
Is activated by every improvement you play, even if you play several in a single turn.

Using both Merchant and Traveling Salesman, you can play up to 4 minor improvements for 1 food on a "major or minor improvement" action space, or you can acquire 2 major improvements for 1 food on a "minor improvement" action space.

Using both Merchant and Businessman, you can first play 1 minor improvement and 1 major or minor improvement, and then additionally for 1 food, either 2 minor improvements or 1 major and 1 minor improvement when you use the "Starting Player" action space.
If you receive goods or food from the first improvement, you may use them to pay for the use of the merchant, or for buying the second improvement.
Is activated when you use the Scholar to play an improvement.

card_text: When you play this card, you receive 1 Vegetable that you may sow immediately if you have an empty plowed field. (You can Sow just this one Vegetable with this extra action.)
card_rulings: If you sow the received Vegetable, it counts as a "Sow" action.
Sowing the vegetable activates the Fieldsman, Smallholder, Potato Dibber, Liquid Manure, Planter Box and another player's Field Worker.
Sowing the vegetable is optional. You may not sow anything else, including wood on Forester and Copse, or food on Vineyard.
You may sow the vegetable on the Beanfield, Lettuce Patch, or Turnip Field.

card_text: In the Feeding phase of each Harvest, only 2 of your people eat 2 Food each; all others are satisfied with only 1 Food.
card_rulings: You still need to feed newborns with 1 Food.

card_text: You receive 1 Food and 1 Wood whenever any player (including you) builds a baking improvement with a baking symbol.
card_rulings: You can place 1 food and 1 wood on the affected unbuilt major improvements to remind you to take the tokens.
You also receive the food and wood when an improvement is upgraded to a baking improvement.

card_text: In each harvest, the Basketmaker can convert up to 1 Reed to 3 Food.
Pile (from bottom to top) 1 Vegetable, Reed, Clay, Wood, Vegetable, Stone, Grain, Reed on this card. At any time, you may buy the top item for 1 Food.
card_rulings: The resources come from the general supply, and are not part of your personal supply until you buy them.

card_text: You can use the Clay Firer at any time to convert 2/3 Clay to 1/2 Stone.
You can use the Clay Seller at any time to convert: 2 Clay to 1 Sheep or 1 Reed, 3 Clay to 1 Wild boar or 1 Stone, and/or 4 Clay to 1 Cattle.
card_rulings: If you receive animals from the Clay Seller, you may convert them to food with an appropriate improvement without having to make room for them in your farmyard.

card_text: Place 1 Clay on each remaining space for rounds 6 to 14. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Clay.
card_rulings: You do not receive clay for the current round, or any of the earlier rounds.

card_text: Whenever one of your people's actions gives you only Clay, you receive 2 additional Clay.
card_rulings: Is activated when you use an action space on which clay is placed each round. You cannot use such an action space just to use this card if the action space contains no clay.
Is also activated by the action space "Take 1 Building Resource" in 3-player game if you take clay.
Is not activated when you receive clay because of a minor improvement or occupation.
Is activated when you take clay from an action space, and at the same time receive other goods because of a minor improvement or occupation.

card_text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each scoring category where you have scored the maximum 4 points. (The bonus point is also awarded for 4 fenced stables.)
card_rulings: Bonus points from other cards, such as the Brewery or the Schnaps Distillery, are not taken into account when scoring the Lord of the Manor.

card_text: Once you have built a Clay hut, place 1 Food on each remaining Round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food. (If you already have a Clay hut or a Stone house when you play this card, place the Food immediately.
Once during the game, at any time after your Stone house reaches at least 4 rooms, you may extend it by 1 room at no cost.
card_rulings: You may place a room tile on this card to show that you have not yet taken the extension action.
You do not have to use an action space to build a room with the Mason.

card_text: In future, whenever you play an Occupation, you receive 2 Food before you pay the costs of the Occupation.
card_rulings: If you use the Writing Desk, you receive food for both occupations you play.
If you also have the Bookshelf, you can use both to receive 5 food for each occupation you play.

card_text: If, when you play this card or later in the game, you are the last player to have only 2 rooms in your home, you receive 3 Wood, 2 Clay, 1 Reed and 1 Stone.
card_rulings: You choose the moment at which you're paid, but it has to be after the last player (other than you) build his third room and before you build your third room yourself.

card_text: Once you have a stone house, you can pay 1 food at the start of each round to plow (at most) 1 field.
card_rulings: When you use the Plow Driver, you may not use a plow or the Harrow to plow multiple fields.
Food that you receive at the start of a round (e.g., because of the Well) can be used to pay for the field immediately.

card_text: Whenever you use either of the "Plow 1 Field" or "Plow Field and/or Sow" action spaces, you can pay 1 Food to Plow 1 additional field.
card_rulings: You may use one of the plows or the Harrow at the same time as the Plow Maker (unlike the Plow Driver); in that case, you pay 1 food to plow 1 field more than the plow or Harrow allows.

card_text: Whenever you use a Family member's action to take Wood from an Action space, you can leave 1 of the Wood on the space and take 2 Food in exchange.
card_rulings: If you also have the Basket, you can use both cards to leave 3 wood to receive 5 food.
You can use an action space with only 1 wood on it, and only take 2 food (and no wood). This still counts as taking wood, and activates, e.g., the Berry Picker, Wood Cart, and Pieceworker.
If there is no wood on the action space, for example because of the Wood Distributor, you may not use the Mushroom Collector.
Is activated when you use an action space that contains wood because of the Wood Distributor.
Is not activated by the action space "1 Reed, Stone, and Wood" in 5-player game.
The Food is "received", not "taken" (poor wording on the card).

card_text: At the end of the game, you receive 1/3/5/7/9 Bonus points for having 5/6/7/8/9+ Improvements in front of you.
card_rulings: Both major and minor improvements count. Discarded improvements do not.

card_text: In rounds 10 and 12, all other players may not place 1 of their family's offspring (if they have any). This card may only be played until the end of Round 9.
card_rulings: The idle family members do count towards the question whether there is space in the hut or house for family growth.
You yourself may place all your family members.
All family members except for the first two are offspring; not only newborns.
Guests and the occupant of the Reed Hut are not offspring and may always be played.
You are allowed to play this card after round 9, but it has no effect. Remove it from the game immediately after you've played it.

card_text: Pay 2 less Clay to renovate to a Clay hut, and pay 2 less Stone to renovate to a Stone house.
You can renovate your Wooden hut to a Stone house without first needing to renovate it to a Clay hut.
card_rulings: You may not use both the Conservator and the Stone Breaker or the Conservator and the Builder's Trowel to renovate your wooden hut to a stone house out of turn.
The renovation is paid, as usual, with 1 stone per room plus 1 reed, and you still have to take a "Renovate" action to use the Conservator.
Activates the Clay Hut Builder when used.

card_text: Add 5 and 9 to the number of the current round. Place 1 Cattle on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Cattle.
card_rulings: If this card is already in play at the start of a solo game, the current round number is 0. You receive the cattle on rounds 5 and 9.
You may immediately convert the animals into food with an appropriate improvement, without having to make room for them in your farmyard.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Day Laborer" action space, you receive 1 additional Grain. From Round 6, you can choose to receive 1 Vegetable instead.
During each Harvest, if you have at least 4 Sheep during the Breeding phase, you receive 2 lambs instead of 1 as long as you have room for them.
Place 1 Sheep on each of the next 3 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Sheep.
card_rulings: You may immediately convert the animal into food with an appropriate improvement, without having to make room for it in your farmyard.

card_text: Place 1 Reed on the next 4 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Reed.
Whenever you use the "1 Wild Boar" action space, you receive 1 additional Wild boar from the supply. (The "1 Wild Boar" action space is added in Stage 3.)
card_rulings: In the 5-player game, there is an action space that gives players 1 animal of their choice. The Swineherd is not activated by using this space.

card_text: Whenever you build at least 1 fence, you also receive 1 stable which you must build immediately. (This may be built inside or outside the fenced area.)(You do not need to pay any Wood for the stable.)
card_rulings: Is activated when you play the Mini Pasture.
Only 1 free stable is awarded in each turn.
Is activated when you use the Fence Overseer: when you build a stable, you may fence it, after which you have to build another stable. You may not then fence that second stable.
Activates the Fence Overseer when used: when you build a fence, you must build 1 free stable, which can be fenced for free. You may not use the Stablehand again to receive a second free stable.
If you have no unbuilt stables left, or if there is no place in your farmyard where it can be placed, the Stablehand has no effect.
If you use the Hedge Keeper, the additional 3 fences do not activate the Stablehand a second time.

card_text: One (and only one) of your unfenced stables may hold up to 3 animals of the same type.
card_rulings: If you also have the Shepherd's Pipe, that card has no effect on the unfenced stable for which the Stablemaster is used.

card_text: You can use the Quarryman at any time to convert Stone to Food. For each Stone you convert, take 2 Food.
card_rulings: The card is worded poorly: "take" has to be interpreted as "receive".

card_text: Whenever you take Stone with an action, you can also take 1 additional Stone. If you also receive other building resources, this costs you 1 Food.
card_rulings: Is activated when you use an action space on which stone is placed each round. You cannot use such an action space just to use this card if the action space contains no stone.
Is activated by the action space "Take 1 Building Resource" in 3-player game if you take stone.
Is also activated by the action space "1 Reed, Stone, and Wood" in 5-player game and the action space "1 Reed, Stone, and Food" in 4-player game, if you pay 1 food.
You also pay 1 food to use this card if you receive other building resources at the same time from occupations and minor improvements.
Is not activated when you receive stone because of a minor improvement or occupation.
Other building resources are wood, clay, and reed.

card_text: All Improvements, Rooms and Renovations cost 1 Stone less.
card_rulings: For example, you do not have to pay any stone if you play Lumber.
If you buy more than one room, you receive the discount for all rooms. If you renovate, you only get a discount of 1 stone, no matter how many rooms your house has.
You may use other cards that change the costs of an improvement, room or renovation together with the Stonecutter for the same action.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Traveling Players" action on an Action space, you receive at least 4 Food. (If there are 1 to 3 Food on the space, take Food from the general supply until you total 4 Food.).
card_rulings: If another player uses the Juggler with a "Traveling Players" action, he pays you 1 food.
If you have the Storyteller as well, you receive at least 4 food and 1 vegetable when you leave one food on the action space.
You cannot use both the Juggler and the Dancer for the same action.

card_text: You receive 1 Cattle when you build your second stable, 1 Wild boar when you build your third and 1 Sheep when you build your fourth. (If you build several stables at once, you may be entitled to take several animals as well.)
card_rulings: You do not get animals for stables that have already been built when you play the Stockman.
You may immediately convert the animal into food with an appropriate improvement, without having to make room for them in your farmyard.

card_text: You receive 1 Cattle when you build your second stable, 1 Wild boar when you build your third and 1 Sheep when you build your fourth. (If you build several stables at once, you may be entitled to take several animals as well.)
card_rulings: In each Harvest, the Potter can convert up to 1 Clay to 2 Food.

card_text: You immediately receive a loan of one of each type of animal. Before scoring, return the 3 animals. For each animal that you cannot or do not want to return, you lose 1 point.
card_rulings: You may immediately convert the animals to food with an appropriate improvement without having to make room for them in your farmyard.
If you also have the Yeoman Farmer, you do not deduct points if you do not return an animal.
If you deduct points for not returning an animal, you do not get the bonus points from the Constable.
If you also have the Animal Feed, you may use the animals from the Animal Feed to repay your loan, even if you have no space in your farm to keep them.

You may repay the loan at any time before the scoring, including during the game. If you repay part of the loan during the game, you may no longer repay the rest of the loan later in the game or before scoring.

card_text: You can keep Sheep, Wild boar and Cattle in the same pasture. This applies to all of your pastures (except the Forest Pasture).
Take 1/2/3/4 Wood if there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play. At the end of the game, all players with the most played Occupation cards receive 3 Bonus points each.
card_rulings: Discarded Occupations do not count for the Reeve bonus.
The Academic counts as two Occupations.
The card text is worded poorly: "Take" here means "Receive".

card_text: To extend your home, you need only 3 of the appropriate resource and 2 Reed for each new room. (For example, if you live in a Wooden hut, you need 3 Wood and 2 Reed.)
card_rulings: Can be used for every room you build, if you build more than 1 room.

This card sets a new base cost for a room. If you also have another card that sets a new base cost (e.g., Clay Supports, Axe, or Clay Plasterer), you may only use one such card at the same time. If you build more than one room, you can choose the same or a different card for each room.
After you've used the Carpenter, you may then also use any card that changes the cost of a room, e.g., Clay Roof, Ladder, Stonecutter, and Bricklayer.
For example, if you also have the Bricklayer, you may use both cards to build rooms for 1 clay and 2 reed.
You may not use this card to change the costs of the Wooden Hut Extension and Clay Hut Extension.

card_text: You receive 4 Wood when you acquire the Joinery or play the Cabinetmaker. When you acquire the Pottery or play the Potter, you receive 4 Clay. When you acquire the Basketmaker's Workshop or play the Basketmaker, you receive 3 Reed. If you have already played any of these cards when you play the Guildmaster, you receive 2 building resources of the appropriate type for each existing card.
card_rulings: If you have already upgraded the Joinery to the Sawmill before you play the Guildmaster, you also get 2 wood.
You receive the building resources after acquiring or playing each card; you cannot use these resources to pay for the cards.

If you e.g. obtain both the Joinery and the Cabinetmaker, you get 4 wood twice. If you play an improvement for a second time (e.g., the Joinery after upgrading it to a Sawmill), you receive the wood again.

card_text: Whenever you Sow 1 field, place 2 additional goods from the supply on it. Whenever you Sow 2 fields, place 1 extra good from the supply on each. (If you sow three or more fields, there is no advantage.)
card_rulings: The extra goods are of the same type as the good(s) you sow.
You also get the extra goods if planting on the Forester, Copse, Lettuce Patch, Beanfield, Turnip Field, Acreage, and Vineyard.
The Acreage counts as two fields if both fields of the Acreage are planted. The Forester counts as up to three fields.
If you use the Scarecrow to sow a field twice, it counts as two fields.
