card_text: Whenever you use a family member's action to take wood that is on an accumulating action space and you have 1/2/3 wild boar, you receive 1/2/3 additional food.
card_rulings: Is not activated when you use the "1 Reed, Stone, and Wood" action space in the 5-player game.
Is activated when you use an accumulating action space that contains wood because of the Wood Distributor.

If you use an action space that contains wood, and you leave all the wood on the action space because of Basket, Mushroom Collector, or Pig Catcher, you still receive the food from the Truffle Digger.
You may first take a wild boar with Pig Catcher before you use the Truffle Digger.

card_text: Whenever an action space is occupied by exactly one of your family members, you may use that action space again with a second family member.
card_rulings: Newborns count as family members, so you cannot use this card for a family growth action space.

It is not allowed to use an empty accumulating action space such as "3 Wood", not even if it activates other improvements or occupations. It is allowed to use an empty accumulating action space that produces other resources directly, such as "1 Reed, Stone, and Wood" in 5-player game.

card_text: For each grain or vegetable you convert to food (in any manner), you receive 1 additional food. You may not convert animals to food for the rest of the game.
card_rulings: You may no longer use a cooking improvement, the Barbecue, Meat-seller, Butcher, Action Artist, or Butcher's Block8 to convert animals to food. You may still receive food from the Slaughterman, Slaughterhouse, Milking Stool, etc.

card_text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 wood. At the end of the game, all players who have the most plowed fields receive 3 bonus points each.
card_rulings: Fields on cards, such as Beanfield and Forester, do not count. Fields with more than one stack of grain because of the Scarecrow count as only 1 field.

card_text: Whenever you receive food from an action space, you receive 1 additional food. This benefit does not apply to food gained due to an improvement or occupation.
card_rulings: Is activated when you use a minor improvement that is an action space, e.g., the Tavern or Spielefest.
Is activated when you use an action space that contains food because of the Foreman.
Is not activated when you use the Net Fisherman.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Fishing" Action space in Round 7/9/11/13, you receive 1 additional Sheep/Wild boar/Cattle animal of your choice.
At the end of the Field phase of each Harvest, you may buy 1 or 2 animals. Pay 2/3/4 Food for each Sheep/Wild boar/Cattle.
card_rulings: You may not use food converted from the purchase of one animal to purchase another in the same Harvest.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Take 1 Grain" or "Take 1 Vegetable" Action space, you receive an additional 1 Wood and 1 Food.

When you build an Oven, pay 1 Building resource less of your choice. Whenever you build a "baking symbol" Improvement, you receive 1 Grain before building the Improvement. (An Oven is a "baking symbol"-Improvement with "Oven" in the name.)

When you play this card, immediately build 1 stable without paying Wood and place it on its side. The stable loses its regular animal functions. (It is still worth 1 additional point if fenced.) In the Feeding phase of each harvest, you receive 1 Food as long as the stable is on your farm.

When you play this card, you receive 7 Food and 2 Begging cards. At any time, you may pay 3 Food to discard one of these 2 Begging cards or a Begging card you received instead of paying 1 Food to feed your family.
card_rulings: You may only use Borrower to discard Begging cards that you received from Borrower or from failing to feed your family in the feeding phase of a harvest.
The ambiguous wording of the English text of the card should not be construed to mean that you can do something in place of feeding your family with this card.

card_text: Whenever you use Clay or Stone for an Extension, Renovation, or Improvement, you receive 1 Wood from the general supply before paying its costs.
card_rulings: If you receive a wood from Branch Collector, then you must spend a Clay or Stone in the subsequent improvement, extension or renovation action. If you have cards that allow for discounts or alternate payments, then you must take these into account. For example, you cannot receive wood if you have Bricklayer and play Flagon.

card_text: Once any player has built an Oven, place 1 Food on the Remaining round spaces. At the start of the rounds, you receive the Food. (If an Oven has already been built, place the Food immediately. If multiple Ovens are built, you only receive the Food once.)

Whenever you use a person's action to take Wood from a Building resource Accumulation space, you may immediately place 1 Clay from your supply on another Building resource Accumulation space to receive 2 Food.
card_rulings: You may use the Chemist on the "Take 1 Reed, also 1 Stone and 1Wood" action space in 5-player game.
If Clay and Wood are both taken from a Building resource Accumulation space, you may use the Clay for Chemist.

card_text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for each of the 5 columns of your farmyard that is either all rooms, all fields, or all in the same pasture.
card_rulings: A single pasture that spans multiple columns counts for each column, as long as the entire column is that pasture.

card_text: Whenever you build a room, renovate your home, or build a Major Improvement, you can replace up to 1 Building resource of your choice with 1 Food.
card_rulings: If you build multiple rooms, you may use the contractor's ability once per room.

card_text: Once each round, during one of your turns, you may take 1 Building resource from the general supply of a type that you do not have and every other player does have.

You can use the "Take 1 Sheep", "Wild boar" or "Cattle" Action spaces, even if they are occupied. If you do this, take 1 animal of that type. Additionally, you can keep 2 animals of the same type on 1 of your Unused farmyard spaces. (The space still counts as used.)

Whenever you remove the last Grain/Vegetable from a field, you may immediately sow a Vegetable/Grain in that field. When you play this card, immediately take 1 Grain or Vegetable from each of your fields and place them in your supply.
card_rulings: You also take Grain or Vegetables from cards such as Turnip Field, Lettuce Patch, Acreage, etc.

If you harvest the last Grain/Vegetable from a card that cannot hold another type of good, (Turnip Field, Beanfield, etc.,) you may not Sow onto that card using Crop Rotator ability. However, if the card can be sown with both Grain or Vegetables, (Cash Crop) then you may sow onto that card using Crop Rotator.
If you also have a card that allows you to take goods from another player's fields (Harvest Helper) you may not use Crop Rotator to sow into that player's fields.

card_text: When you renovate your Clay hut, you receive 2 Clay afterward. Once you live in a Clay hut or Stone house, whenever another player renovates their Wooden/Clay hut, you receive 2 Wood/Clay.
Once during round 14, you may use an Action space even if it is occupied by other players. If you play this card in Round 7 or before, you may do this twice instead.
Whenever you play an Improvement, you receive 1 Food before paying the costs of the Improvement. If the Improvement has a printed Stone cost, you receive 2 Food instead of 1.
card_rulings: Whenever you play an Minor Improvement, you receive 1 Food before paying the costs of the Improvement. If the Improvement has a printed Stone cost, you receive 2 Food instead of 1.

card_text: Whenever any player (including you) builds 1 or more rooms, they must place 1 resource of those paid on this card. You may use these resources to pay for your extensions or renovations.
card_rulings: If a player extends without paying resources for the extension (using Wooden Hut Extension, Remodeling, Remodeler, etc.,) then no resources go on Expert Builder.
Expert Builder may be used with Stone Crane when building or renovating.
Resources placed on Expert Builder or used from Expert Builder are never received, therefore they do not trigger things like Resource Seller.

card_text: Add 4, 7 and 10 to the current round and place up to 1 person still in your supply on each corresponding Round space. At the start of these rounds and not earlier, you may take the person as a new Family member, if you have room. (If you don't, return it to your supply.) It counts as a Newborn, and it may take actions the following round.
card_rulings: Taking the person counts as a "Family Growth" action.
May combine with cards that allow Family Growth without space in your house.

card_text: You may only play this card as your first Occupation. From now on, you may not use any Occupation action on any Action space. At the start of each Harvest, you may pay 2 Food to play an Occupation.
card_rulings: You may still play occupations in other ways, for example with Apprenticeship or Farm School.

card_text: You may only build 12 fences. ~~(Remove the other 3 fences from the game.)~~ Whenever you build fences, you may build 2 fences for every 1 Wood paid.
card_rulings: Card received errata to remove the parenthetical "(Remove the other 3 fences from the game.)"
Does trigger with Hops Field, Pest Exterminator, Fence Builder, Moor Archaeology, etc.

card_text: Pile (from bottom to top) 1 Clay, 1 Vegetable, 1 Stone, 1 Grain, 1 Wood and 1 Reed on this card. Whenever you use a person to take Food from the "Day Laborer", "Fishing" or "Traveling Players" Action space, additionally take the top item on this card.

Place 3 Arrow markers on this card. Each round, between the Work and Returning home phase, you may pay 1 Wood and return 1 marker to move one of your people from the "Plow 1 field" or "Plow 1 field and/or Sow" Action space to a free Action space and take the action.
card_rulings: If the Countryman, Pond Watchman, or Acrobat also have been played, the family members are moved in player order, starting with the player left of the one who placed the last regular person. If you can move more than one person, you move them consecutively in an order of your choice. You cannot move the same person twice. You cannot move to an action space that was occupied after the last regular person placement.

card_text: Whenever you use a person to take Clay from a Building resource Accumulation space, you may also receive 1 Stone. In Round 9 or before, if there are 3 or more Clay on the space, you must leave 1 Clay on the space to receive the Stone.
Whenever you use an Action space orthogonally adjacent to an occupied "Family Growth" Action space from stage 2 or stage 5, you receive 1 Grain immediately before the action is resolved.
card_rulings: If a "Family Growth" Round card is in a stage other than 2 or 5 (for example, due to Seeress), orthogonal spaces will still trigger Godfather.

card_text: Whenever you build fences, you receive 1 Bonus point. When you play this card, you immediately get a "Build fences" action.
card_rulings: The bonus points should be recorded on your score sheet. Therefore, if you remove the Hedge Master, you still receive the bonus points.

card_text: At the start of round 6/10/13, you may ~~(before phase 1)~~ build 1 Wooden/Clay/Stone room if your home is made of the same material. Pay 1 less Clay for the Clay room, and 2 less Stone for the Stone room. (When you play this card, place the Room tiles on the corresponding round spaces.)
card_rulings: Card received errata to remove the parenthetical "(before phase 1)" clause.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Day Laborer" Action space, you receive 1 additional Sheep. From round 6, you can choose to receive 1 Wild boar instead.
Whenever you build 1 or more fences or Plow 1 or more fields, you receive 2/1 Food if you have exactly 2/3 rooms.
card_rulings: If you have Downsizing, count the number of rooms that you have after removing one.

card_text: Each Harvest, you can place 1 Building resource on this card from your supply. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3/5 Bonus points for 1/2/3/4 different resources on this card.
card_rulings: You receive 1/2/3/4 bonus points for 1/2/3/4 different resources on this card.

card_text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 Wood. At the end of the game, all players with the fewest cards in front of them receive 3 Bonus Points. (Count Occupations plus Improvements and exclude Begging cards.)
card_rulings: Counts the number of cards. Therefore, cards like Academic and Clockwork Man still count as 1 card each.

card_text: Whenever you receive animals outside the Breeding phase of a Harvest, you may immediately convert them into Food. Take 2/3/4 Food for each Sheep/Wild Boar/Cattle converted.
card_rulings: When you use Sheep Farmer to convert Sheep to Wild Boar and Cattle, you may use Hunter to convert the Wild Boar and Cattle into Food.
Animals gained from breeding outside the Breeding phase (Cattle Breeder, Ram, etc.,) may be converted to food with Hunter.

card_text: If you play this card during Stage 1, place 1 person still in your supply on Round 12. At the start of this round, you can pay 2 Food to move this person into your home whether or not you have room. It counts as an adult Family member and may take actions starting Round 12.
card_rulings: Does not count as a family growth action.

card_text: When you play this card, you receive 1 Wild boar. If you already have a Wild boar, you receive 1 Cattle instead. In the Breeding phase of each Harvest, you may pay 1 Food to receive 2 offsprings of exactly 1 animal type that breeds instead of 1.
card_rulings: Does not trigger with horses.

card_text: Place 2 Wood, 2 Clay, 1 Reed, and 1 Stone on four different revealed Action spaces respectively (occupied or unoccupied.) Afterward, any player (including you) who uses one of these spaces, may also take the extra resource(s) by paying you 1 Food first.
card_rulings: If a resource is taken, it is received before the Action that it is on occurs, and can be used to pay costs for the Action.

card_text: Each round, instead of placing your first Family member on an Action space, you may place it on this card and receive 2 Food and 1 Guest token to place that round. (You may not do this if you will be placing more people after your first without another player placing in between.)
card_rulings: This card is not an action space.
You cannot double the Master Cook benefit (e.g., using Golem or Sunday Worker.)
Using Master Cook does not count as an action (Church Warden).
If a Family member placed on Master Cook has placement restrictions (Shelter in the Field, etc.,) then the Guest token also has the same restrictions.

card_text: Whenever you use a person's action to take 2 or more animals from an Accumulation space, you can also build up to 2 stables, at a cost of 1 Wood each, before taking the animals.
At the end of the game, you receive 9/6/4/2 Bonus points for having exactly 0/1/2/3 Improvements in front of you.

At any time, you may convert all the printed Victory points (if any) of an Improvement in front of you to 3 Food per point. Afterward, you may not return or remove the Improvement and you no longer receive its Victory points. If the Pawnbroker gets turned face down or discarded, you still lose the points.
card_rulings: You may use the Pawnbroker with any number of improvements.

card_text: You may sow Grain on Unused farmyard spaces. Planted spaces count as Grain fields and do not need to be adjacent to your existing plowed fields. You may move Grain from these fields to the general supply at any time and you must move Grain before scoring. (Or if this card gets removed or turned face down).
card_rulings: You may not use Reed Nursery to sow Reed onto Unused farmyard spaces.
Sown Grain in Unused farmyard spaces do not count as Grain fields for prerequisites.
Does not combine with Planter Box or Scarecrow.
Farmyard spaces containing sown grain count as used.

card_text: Twice each round, you may exchange 1 Reed for 1 Stone, or 1 Stone for 1 Reed. When you play this card, you receive 1 Stone.
card_rulings: You may convert 1 Reed to 1 Stone and later 1 Stone to 1 Reed (or vice versa) in the same round.

card_text: When you renovate your Wooden hut, you may first return 1 built stable to extend your hut by 1 Wooden room for free before paying the costs of the Renovation. (The stable can be rebuilt later.)
card_rulings: The stable must be returned from your farmyard.
May combine with Clay Starter.

card_text: Whenever you receive Building resources from an Action space, instead of placing them in your supply, you may add 1 resource of 1 type received and place them all on the next Round spaces with 1 per space (in any order and discard any excess.) At the start of these rounds, you receive the resource.
card_rulings: If a resource is bought with Reed Buyer, Wood Buyer or Stone Buyer, you may still use Resource Lender to add an additional good of that type.

card_text: Once you live in a Stone house, place 1 Grain on each remaining even Round space and 1 Vegetable on each remaining odd Round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the resources. (If you already live in a Stone house, place the resources immediately.)

Add 3, 5, 7, and 9 to the current round and place up to 1 of your unbuilt stables on each remaining corresponding Round space. At the start of these rounds you may pay 1 Wood to build that stable immediately. (If you don't, return that stable to your supply. Until then, you cannot build that stable.)

Whenever you use a Stone Accumulation space, you receive 1 additional Food. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for every 2 rooms in your Stone house and every 2 Improvements with a printed Stone cost.

When you play this card, you may place 1 to 4 Building resources of different types on it from your supply. At the start of each Harvest, choose up to 2 resource types currently on this card and add 1 of each from the general supply. Each round, you may take all the resources of any 1 type remaining.
card_rulings: Resources on this card do not count towards Storehouse Clerk. At the end of the game, resources on this card count toward Sawmill, Joinery}, Pottery}, etc.

card_text: Once during Rounds 7 and 14, you may pay 1 Food to take one of your peoples' actions twice. If you use an Accumulation space with that action, you get double the amount of Goods and/or Food.
card_rulings: You may double an action immediately after the action is taken. You cannot wait until later in the round to double an earlier action.
If you play Sunday Worker with an action in Round 7 or 14, you may pay 1 Food to double that action.
You may use Food collected from taking the action once to pay for Sunday Worker.

On any Accumulation space, double the amount of Goods and/or Food taken, regardless of type. For example, food left on an Accumulation space by Wood Worker or Foreman will be doubled. On a non-Accumulation space, do not double these resources, just take the action twice.
A doubled action only counts once for Church Warden.
If you also have Golem, apply the Sunday Worker effect first, then the Golem effect.

card_text: At the start of the next 3 rounds, you may pay 2 Food to Plow 1 of the three Eastern-most spaces on your farm (the farm side opposite your home) if they are unused. (Normal plowing rules still apply.) If you choose not to, discard the Sunrise Admirer.
card_rulings: If you have changed the shape of your farm (for example, from Farm Extension) the Sunrise Admirer will still plow 1 of the Eastern-most spaces for the next 3 rounds from when you played it.
If the Eastern-most spaces are completely plowed (or otherwise blocked) then you cannot plow anymore.

card_text: Whenever you have at least 3 Sheep during the Field phase of a Harvest, place 1 Food from the general supply in your home. You may only place 1 Food per room. You cannot use these Food, but each is worth 1 Bonus point at the end of the game.
card_rulings: Different cards (such as Pelts or Cookies) may put food in the rooms. The 1 Food maximum is for each such card.
If you remove a room with a Food on it (for example, with Downsizing) you also lose the Bonus point that Food represents.

card_text: At the start of each Harvest, receive 1 Food and 1 Grain if you have at least 1 room in your home that is not occupied by a Family member. At the end of the game, receive 1 Bonus Point for every empty room.
If there are 1/3/6/9 rounds left to play you receive 1/2/3/4 Clay. At the end of the game all players with a Clay hut receive 2 Bonus points. (This card does not give Bonus points for a Stone house.)

At the start of each round, you may place 1 card face down from your hand under Village Fool. That card can no longer be used for the rest of the game. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus points for each card under Village Fool except the 3rd, 5th, 7th and 9th.
card_rulings: Cards face down are front of you, it does not trigger with Minimalist and Humble Farmboy.

card_text: Whenever you use a person's actions to take exactly 1 resource from a Building resource Accumulation space, you receive an additional 1 Building resource of your choice or 1 Food. In a 4/5 player game, you instead receive an additional 1 Building resource of your choice and 1 Food.
card_rulings: Triggers as long as you take exactly 1 resource from an Accumulation space. Other cards may alter the amount of resources you receive (such as Mushroom Collector, Wood Buyer, Pieceworker etc.)

If you take exactly 1 Reed from a Building resource Accumulation space, and you have Landing Net, you gain 2 food from the Landing Net, regardless of other resources received from Wheelbarrow Pusher. (According the the Landing Net compendium v9.0 ruling, you only get 1 Food if you take a resource other than Reed. However, this particular ruling is controversial and contrary to many similar rulings.)
Wheelbarrow Pusher is not triggered when you use the "Take 1 Reed, also 1 Stone and 1 Wood" action space in 5-player game.
If you take 1 Reed and use Wheelbarrow Pusher to receive a Stone (or vice versa), it does not activate Storehouse Keeper.

card_text: Whenever you use any Action space, immediately afterward you may place 1 Food from your supply on the space to receive 1 Wood. The next player to use the Action space receives this food.
card_rulings: Placing the Food to receive Wood happens after the Action is completed, and therefore the Wood cannot be used to pay costs for that Action.

card_text: At the start of each round, you may place 1 Food from your supply on the "Plow 1 field" Action space. Any player that uses that space also receives the Food. At the end of the round, if the space has not been used, you take the Food back and may Plow 1 field.
At the end of each Work phase in which you used both the "Build Stables" action on an Action space and the "Build Fences" action on an Action space, you receive 1 animal of each kind.
card_rulings: You must use the actions on an action space, so this card does not trigger when fencing using Hedge Master or Mini Pasture, or alternatively while building stables using Stable Deliveryman.
It does not combo with Fence Builder.
You can not fence with "Renovation and Fences" action, you must use "Build Fences" action.
Excluding horse.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Traveling Players" Action, you receive an additional 1 Food and a building resource of your choice.
card_rulings: If you also have played the Dancer, you receive at least 5 food when using the "Traveling Players" action (see other "Traveling Players" compendium entries).
Whenever you use the "Traveling Players" Action, you receive an additional 1 Food and a building resource of your choice (wood, clay or reed).

card_text: Once per round, when you build a Major or Minor Improvement, you may pay 1 additional building resource of those already paid for 1 Bonus Point.
card_rulings: Up to a maximum of 7 bonus points.

card_text: Once during the game, when you have 4 fenced pastures, you may return 1 animal to the supply to extend your Wooden hut by 1 room at no cost.
card_rulings: This counts as "Building a room".

card_text: Whenever you use the "Fishing" Action space, you may place 1 Grain from the supply on each of your empty plowed fields. These fields are now considered planted fields. (This does not count as a "Sow" action)
card_rulings: Empty plowed fields are field tiles on your farmyard that do not have any goods on them.
Up to a maximum of 3 fields.
The fields with 1 Grain are not Grain fields, it does not combo with Sleeping Corner, Straw-thatched Roof, Free-Range Chickens,Festival Hall, and Plum Patch.

card_text: When you play this card, you receive 1 Vegetable. For each Vegetable that you convert into Food, you can place 1 Clay from your personal supply on this card. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for each Clay on this card, up to a maximum of 5 points.
card_rulings: Up to a maximum of 4 bonus points.

card_text: From bottom to top, stack 1 Stone, Reed, Clay, and Wood on this card. At the end of each round, move the top resource to the bottom of the stack. Whenever you receive the top/bottom resource type, take 1 additional/fewer resource of that type. (Take from or move the resource to the supply).
If there are 1/2/4/7 animals on your farm, you may pay 1/2/3/4 fewer building resources of your choice for each new Wooden room.
Whenever you fence a new pasture that covers at least 3 farmyard spaces, you can pay 1 Reed to receive 2 Cattle.
card_rulings: The number of 3 (or greater) farmyard space pastures must increase to activate this card.
If you fence more than one new 3 space pastures at once, you may convert multiple reed to obtain more cattle.

card_text: Whenever another player uses a "Family Growth" action, you may convert 1 Wood to 2 Food, or 1 Wood and 1 Reed to 5 Food.
Whenever you use the "Day Laborer" Action space, you can also play a Minor Improvement or pay 1 Food to play a Major Improvement.
card_rulings: Pay for the improvement costs normally.
If you have played Quarry, you can use the stone to play for the improvement.

card_text: Starting from next round, you cannot take actions with 1 of your Family members. At the end of the game, you receive 2 Bonus Points for every round after playing this card, including the current round.
card_rulings: You must still feed the convict as normal.
If you have played other cards which require you to play one (or more) fewer family members, you cannot use one person to satisfy both Convict Number 24601 and the other card.

card_text: Once during the game, when you have 3 planted Grain fields, you may play the "Family growth" action as "Family growth even without room".
card_rulings: The fields must be planted when the Family Growth action is taken.
Once during the game, when you have 2 planted Grain fields and 1 planted Vegetable field, you may play the "Family growth" action as "Family growth even without room".

card_text: From Round 4 on, at the end of any Work phase in which you used 2 or fewer Action spaces and have 2 or fewer Family members, you receive 1 Food. From Round 8/12, you may choose to receive an animal/Plow 1 field instead.
card_rulings: You are allowed to use additional actions at the end of, or after the work phase and still activate this card (Forest Farmer, Acrobat, etc).
In the round you use family growth, then you will have more than 2 family members and Cowboy And Mother will not activate.
If you use the Guest (or obtain additional actions some other way) then you cannot activate Cowboy And Mother.

card_text: When you play this card, place a Guest token on any Round space at least 3 higher than the current round. At the start of that round, pay the Starting player (which may be you) 1 Food and place the Guest. After that, play starts with the starting player as usual.
card_rulings: Debt Collector counts as an action towards the Church Warden bonus.
Add 3 to the current round and pay the Starting player 2 Food to place the Guest.

card_text: In the Feeding phase of each Harvest, the Chandler can convert either 1 Sheep to 4 Food or 1 Cattle to 6 Food.
card_rulings: When eating an animal with the Dove Hunter, you can place a food with Pelts.

card_text: Immediately after using the most recent Round card, you may play 1 Major or Minor Improvement. (Pay costs for the Improvement normally.)
Place 1 Food each on the spaces for rounds 7 to 14. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.
card_rulings: You do not receive food for the current round, or any of the earlier rounds.

card_text: Whenever you play an Occupation (including this one), place up to 2 fences from your supply on this card. From your 4th Occupation on, place up to 1 fence instead. You build these fences for free when taking a "Fences" Action.
card_rulings: Your 4th occupation is the 4th occupation you have played, not 4th occupation after playing this card.
You cannot build the additional fences when "fencing one space in your farmyard" but only when taking the "fences" action (so no combo with Mini Pasture).

card_text: At the end of each round in which you did not receive any Building resources, you receive 1 Wood. If its an evennumbered round, you additionally receive 1 Food.
card_rulings: If there are multiple effects which occur "at the end of the round" you may choose the order (i.e., receive resources at end of round after activating Fiddler).
If you harvest any Building resources during fileds phase, you can not activate Fiddler.
"its" should read "it's".

card_text: You immediately receive a loan of 1 Grain and 1 Vegetable. Before scoring, return the 2 crops from your supply. For each crop that you cannot or choose not to return, you receive 1 Negative point.
card_rulings: If you also have the Yeoman Farmer, you do not deduct points if you do not return a crop.
If you deduct points for not returning a crop, you do not get the bonus points from the Constable.
You may repay the loan at any time before scoring. If you repay part of the loan during the game, you may no longer repay the rest later in the game or before scoring.
The gold bonus point icon at the bottom is a misprint, it should be a dark (or negative) bonus point symbol.

card_text: Place 3 Wood, 2 Clay, 2 Stone, and 1 Reed on this card. Whenever another player pays building resources to build a Minor Improvement, you may take 1 building resource of the same type from this card.
card_rulings: Is activated when another player builds a major as a minor (Well Builder, Goblet, Manufacturer, etc).
You can only take resources that were paid by the other player.

card_text: Add 1, 3, and 5 to the current round and place 1 Grain on each corresponding Round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Grain.
If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 Wood. At the end of the game, all players who harvest at least 5 goods during the final Harvest receive 3 Bonus points.
card_rulings: Goods must be harvested during the harvest to be counted.
Only the number of goods harvested is important, not the number of fields.

card_text: If there are still 1/3/6/9 rounds to play, you immediately receive 1/2/3/4 Wood. After playing this card, the next player to have no Unused farmyard spaces (who currently has Unused spaces) receives 3 Bonus points.
card_rulings: A player with no unused spaces when the card is played is ineligible for the bonus.
If a plow is used to fill the spaces when the Punner is in play, the Punner activates after the plow is completed (Kimball, forum).
If there are multiple events that happen "at the same time" or "immediately" then go in turn order, starting from Start Player (Kimball, forum).

card_text: When you play your 5th, 6th and 7th Occupation, you can Plow 1 field immediately afterward. (You cannot Plow immediately if you play this card as your 5th, 6th, or 7th Occupation.)
Once you have a Stone house, you can pay 1 Food at the start of each round for 1 animal of your choice.
When you Sow, you may Sow on this card as if it were 2 fields. When you play this card, you may take a "Sow" action. (This card does not count as a field when scoring.)
Twice per round, you may exchange 1 Grain from your supply for 1 building resource, or 1 Vegetable from your supply for 2 different building resources.
card_rulings: When you convert a vegetable, the two building resources you receive must be different.

card_text: At the end of each round in which you received exactly 1 type of building resource (in any way), you also receive 1 more building resource of that type.
At the start of each Harvest, you may discard up to 2 Minor Improvements from your hand to receive 2 Food each.
At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus Point for each card with a Bonus point symbol that you play after this one. (An example of a Bonus point symbol appears on the bottom of this card.)
card_rulings: Each instance of playing a card with a bonus symbol gives you a bonus point (for example if you play Joinery, return it, then rebuy it you get 2 points).
The "grey" or "negative" version of the symbol also counts towards Mastermind.
Tavern and Clay Deposit count as they have a bonus point symbol on the card.

card_text: Whenever you use a "Build Room(s)" action on an Action space, you may pay 1 Wood/ Clay/ Stone less and 1 Reed less if you build exactly 1 Wood/ Clay/ Stone room. (It is irrelevant whether you build stables.)
card_rulings: You only get the discount if you use the action on an action space to build exactly one room.

card_text: Place an Arrow marker in the intersection between 4 Action spaces. At the end of each Work phase, if 2/3/4 of those spaces are occupied by your Family members, you receive 1 Sheep / Wild boar / Cattle.
Pile (from bottom to top) 1 Vegetable, 1 Stone, 1 Grain, 1 Clay, 1 Wood on this card. Whenever you Plow exactly 1 field, you may receive the top item.
card_rulings: If you plow more than one field at a time (with a plow or the harrow) you do not receive any benefit from Oceanographer.
If you plow more than one field, but each plow action is distinct (for example, Sunday Worker), then you get a good from Oceanographer.

card_text: At the end of each Work phase in which you reduced the number of your unused farmyard spaces, you receive 1 Wood. If you reduced it by 2 or more spaces, you also receive 1 Food.
card_rulings: The check for Parquet Setter occurs at the end of the work phase, so you must have a net reduction in unused farmyard spaces from the start of the work phase.

card_text: Pile (from bottom to top) 1 Cattle, Sheep, Wild boar, Sheep on this card. You receive the top item when you receive that type of animal outside of the Breeding phase of Harvest.
card_rulings: If you receive multiple animals at the same time, you can choose the order.

card_text: Whenever you use an action to take Wood from an Action space, you may leave 1/2/3 Wood on the space and take 1 Grain/ Vegetable/ Grain and Vegetable in exchange.
card_rulings: If you also have the Mushroom Collector, you can leave behind 2 wood to receive 2 food and a grain (similar with Basket).
You can use an action space with only 1 wood on it, and only take a grain (and no wood). This still counts as taking wood, and activates Berry Picker, Wood Cart, Pieceworker).

If there is less than 3 wood on the action space (for example because of the Wood Distributor) you may not use the Pear Peeler to get a grain and a vegetable; not even if you receive additional wood from the Wood Cart.

card_text: Whenever you have at least 1 planted Grain field at the start of a Harvest, you receive 2 Wood.
Whenever you have at least 3/5 Stone in your supply at the start of the Work phase, you receive 1/2 Food.
card_rulings: You can pick up stone from the "start of round" and then activate Powerhouse, or alternatively activate Politician to convert a reed to a stone and then activate Powerhouse.

card_text: You receive 1 Food for each Occupation and for each Improvement used by any player to convert building resources to Food during Harvest.
If you have fewer Improvements than 2/3/4 other players, you may pay 1/2/3 building resource less of your choice when you play an Improvement.
card_rulings: You must have fewer improvements than 2 other players to get any benefit from Prosecutor.

card_text: Whenever you build at least 1 stable, you may also pay 1 Food to immediately Plow 1 field.
You may keep any 1 animal on each of your played Occupation cards.
card_rulings: The animals count as on your farm.

card_text: Place 4 stacks of resources on this card: 3 Wood, 3 Clay, 2 Stone, and 1 Grain with 1 Vegetable. In the Field phase of each Harvest you can buy (some or all of) these stacks. Pay 2/3/4/5 Food for the 1st/ 2nd/ 3rd/ 4th stack you buy.
If you have the Joinery/ Pottery/ Basketmaker's Workshop or their upgrades, you receive an additional 2 Wood/ 2 Clay/ 1 Reed whenever you use a Family member's action to take only Wood/ Clay/ Reed.
card_rulings: The upgrades include any minor improvement that requires you to return the corresponding major and give up and bonus points for a repurchased major.
Activates so long as you take only one kind of good, even if you receive additional goods through an occupation or improvement (similar to Wheelbarrow).
If there is a wood on the occupation space, then you can after playing it take the wood and activate Sculptors Son.
If there is a food on the space, you cannot activate the Sculptors Son.

card_text: In the Field phase of each Harvest, you receive 1/2/3/4 Food if you have 2/3/4/5 pastures.
If you play this card in Stage 1, you immediately receive 6 wood. Whenever another player uses the "Traveling Player" action, you must pay him 1 Wood if you have any in your supply.
Pile 6 building resources of your choice, but including at least 1 of each type, on this card in any order. During the Field phase of each Harvest, you receive the top item.
Whenever you use the "Take 1 Grain" action, you receive 1 additional Sheep. Whenever you use the accumulating "Sheep" action, you receive 1 additional Grain.
If you are the only player to have only 2 rooms in your home and you use a "Build rooms" action on an Action space to build at least 1 room, you may build 1 additional room for free.
card_rulings: You must use the "Build Room(s)" action on an action space, so this does not combo with any extension minor, Wood Saw, etc.

card_text: At the start of each round, if you have no empty plowed fields on your farm, you may pay 1 Food to Plow 1 field.
card_rulings: You may plow your first field with this card.

card_text: You may immediately build up to 4 fences and 1 stable for free. At the end of the final Harvest, you must return 4 Wood to the general supply. If you cannot, you must take 1 Begging Card for each Wood you cannot return.
card_rulings: The bonus point symbol is an error and should be disregarded (Kimball, forum).

card_text: At the start of each round, you may pay 1 Stone to place 1 of your unused fences as a road between 2 orthogonally adjacent Action spaces. At the end of each Work phase, you may use 1 empty Action space with a road to one occupied by you.
card_rulings: A person that uses Unicycle Driver can also be moved with Forest Farmer, Pond Watchman, etc (so long as the person starts on the space that activates the "moving card").
Roads are not contiguous, you can only link one space to one other space with a road (you cannot build long stretch of road).
Unicycle Driver is resolved during the same phase as Forest Farmer, Pond Watchman, etc (which occurs in turn order, starting from the Start Player).
If you have played another card that moves the same family member, you can choose when to activate the Unicycle Driver (before or after moving it).
Unicycle Driver may use unoccupied spaces that were occupied at the end of the work phase.
The additional action, activated by Unicycle Driver, does combo with cards like Wheelbarrow Pusher, Berry Picker, or Clay Mixer.

card_text: In the future, whenever you play an Occupation, you receive 1 Sheep afterward.
If this is your first Occupation, immediately take 1 building resource of your choice for each completed round of the game.
Each round, if you spend 4 or more Wood for Improvements, rooms, stables, or fences, you receive 2 Wood at the end of the round.
card_rulings: If you pay wood and then play Wood Gatherers, you can activate it at the end of the round.

card_text: When you play the card, if there are no animals on your farm, place 5 Wood, 4 Clay, and 3 Stone on this card. At any time, if you have 5/7/9 animals, you receive all the Wood/Clay/ Stone on this card.
card_rulings: You must have room for the animals and store them on your farm to gain resources.
You can take the resources at any time, provided you have the correct number of animals on your farm.
At any time, if you have 6/8/10 animals, you receive all the Wood/Clay/Stone on this card.

card_text: Place 1 Occupation (from your hand) face down on each space for rounds 5,7,9, and 11. At the start of these rounds, you can pay 2 Food to play the Occupation. If you do not, return it to your hand.
card_rulings: You cannot use this in combination with Perpetual Student to play a random occupation at the start of the given rounds.

card_text: At the end of the game, you receive 1 bonus point for each of your pastures that cannot contain any more animals than it does. If all of your pastures qualify, you receive 1 additional bonus point.
card_rulings: To receive the additional point, you need to have at least 1 pasture.
When calculating the capacity of your pastures, you have to take into account fenced stables and the Drinking Trough and Shepherd's Pipe.
You can also get points for Animal Yard and Wildlife Reserve.
You can only receive a point for Night Pasture if all other players have 1 animal on this card (and you 3).
If you have Forest Pasture, you never receive a point for that card or the additional bonus point.
Up to a maximum of 3 bonus points.

card_text: Place 3 vegetables from the general supply on this card. Whenever you use the "Plow 1 Field" Action space, you may pay 1 Food for one of the Vegetables.
card_rulings: The vegetables are not part of your personal supply until you buy them.

card_text: When you play this card, if you only have 2 rooms in your Wooden hut, immediately pay 1 Food to renovate it to Clay for free.
card_rulings: Has no effect if you have more than 2 rooms or live in a clay hut or stone house when you play this card.

card_text: Whenever at the a of a Work phase all of your adult Family members were placed on the same piece of cardboard, you receive 1 Food, if it were 2 Adults. For 3 Adults, you receive 1 Grain. For 4+ Adults, you receive 1 Vegetable.
card_rulings: Newborns do not count, unless they took an action because of Adoptive Parents.
Guests count as family members for this card.
If you move a person, e.g., because of Countryman or Pond Watchman, the location of your family members after you've moved them counts.
Action spaces on minor improvements, or introduced into the game by the Clay Digger or Master Forester, are not on any game board.
The "Infirmary" is a separate game board; if all of your family members take that action, you receive the bonus from the Family Counselor.

card_text: If there are still 7 rounds to play, you may Plow 1 field, which you may immediately Sow with 1 Grain from your supply. At the end of the game, all players with the most Grain receive 2 Bonus points each.
card_rulings: You may plow a field and not sow it.
Sowing the grain counts as a sowing action, and activates, e.g., the Smallholder, Seasonal Plants, and another player's Field Worker.

card_text: Place 1 Wood, 1 Clay, 1 Reed, and 1 Stone on the next 4 Round spaces in any order you choose. At the start of those rounds, you receive the resource.
Between the Field and Feeding phases of each Harvest, you may play a Minor Improvement or pay 2 Food to build a Major Improvement by paying its cost.
card_rulings: Pay the costs of the improvement normally.
A harvest counts as part of the preceding round. If you also have the Wood Carver, you may use it if you didn't already in the round that preceded the harvest.

card_text: When you play this card, if it is your first Occupation, you receive 1 Food for every other Occupation in play (by any player) up to a maximum 7 Food.
If there are still 3/6/9 rounds to play, you may immediately take a "Fences" action and pay 2/3/4 Wood less. At the end of the game, all players with the most pastures receive 2 Bonus points.
card_rulings: When played, may immediately use fence(s) provided by Fencing Master for the "Fences" action.

card_text: Whenever you receive animals from an Action space, immediately afterward you receive 1/2/3 Wood if you have 1/2/3+ people on Action spaces that provide animals.
At the start of each round, you may place 1 Food from your supply on the "Traveling Players" Action space to play a Minor Improvement. (The next player to use the Action space receives the Food.)

You may no longer convert animals to Food. Whenever you use an animal Accumulation space, you receive an additional 3 Food. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for each animal category in which you score the maximum 4 points.

Whenever any player plays an Improvement, they may pay 1 less Stone and pay 1 more Food instead. Whenever another player does this, you receive 1 Food. When you play this card, you may play an Improvement with a printed Stone cost.
For you, the Basket Maker's Workshop is a Minor Improvement and costs only 1 Reed and 1 Stone. In each Harvest, the Braid Maker can convert up to 1 Reed to 2 Food.
Whenever you use the "Traveling Players" or "Fishing" Action space, you may move exactly 1 Food from that space to the other space and receive 1 Cattle.
Whenever you place 2/3 newborn animals on your farm during the Breeding phase of the Harvest, you receive 1/2 Bonus points.
When you play this card, if it is your third Occupation, you may immediately pay 1 Food to play another Occupation and/or immediately play a Minor Improvement by paying its cost.
Whenever another player grows their family, you can pay 2 Food to the supply to play an Occupation.

Place 1 Food from the general supply on up to 3 unused farmyard spaces on all Farms. When the spaces are used, you receive the Food.
card_rulings: May be combined with other cards that place Food on unused spaces, e.g., Raspberries and Pine Cones.

card_text: Once during the game, after using a Stone Accumulation space, you may pay exactly 3 Stone and 1 Reed to build at most 1 Stone Room.
After using a Round 1-4 action space, you receive 1 food. If you paid building resource(s) for a cost when using the space, 1 of those resources returns to your supply.
Place 2 Cattle and 2 Wild Boar on this card. Whenever another player builds an Improvement with a Cooking symbol you receive a Animal from this card.
Place 4 different building resources and 1 Vegetable on 5 different unbuilt Major Improvements. When these Improvements are acquired, you receive the Good.
Whenever you take exactly 3 Food from an Action space, you may Plow 1 field.
When you Renovate your home you receive 3 Food and 1 Reed before taking the action.

When you play this card you receive 1 Reed. Yoy may place each Reed you spend for an extension or renovation on this card. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for every 2 Reed on this card.

When you play this card, you receive 2 Food and 1 building resource of your choice. Whenever 3/4 of your Family members use 3/4 adjacent Action spaces (forming a vertical or horizontal line), you receive 1/3 Bonus points.

When you play this card, you receive 1 Grain. You can use the "Take 1 Grain" and "Sow and/or Bake Bread" Action spaces even if another player has placed a person on the space. When you play this card, you can immediately take a "Bake bread" action.
Each time you use a Wood accumulation space, you also get a "Sow" action for exactly 1 field.
The next time you build at least 1 fence, you recieve 1 Vegetable and 1 Food. Each time you build at least 1 fence after that, you recieve 1 Grain and 1 Food.
Each time you use the "Plow 1 field", "Take 1 Grain", "Sow and/or Bake bread", or "Plow 1 field and/or Sow" action space, at the end of that turn, you get 1 Wood.
When you play this card, you receive 2 Wood. Additionally, place 1 Wood on each of the next round spaces, up to the number of fences you built. At the start of these rounds, you get the Wood.

Pile (from bottom to top) 1 Stone, 1 Clay, 1 Stone, 1 Reed, 1 Wood, and 1 Clay on this card. Whenever you build a room, play an Improvement, or renovate your home, you may use the top resource(s) on this card.
As long as your home has exactly 2 rooms, you receive 1 Wood at the start of each Round.
When you play this card, you can immediately build exactly 1 stable for 1 Wood. Exactly one of your unfenced stables can hold up to 3 animals of one type.
At the start of each Work phase, if you live in a Stone house, you may put 1 of your played cards under this card. It has lost its function, but each gives 1 Bonus point at the end of the game.
When you play this card, you immediately get your choice of 1 wood or 1 grain. Instead of just 1 animal total, you can keep any 1 animal in each room of your house.
You can renovate your wooden house directly to stone without renovating it to clay first.
Each time you take a "Build Fences" action, you do not have to pay wood for 3 of the fences you build.
Once you live in a stone house, at the start of each round, you can pay 1 food to plow 1 field.
For 1 food, you can take an action with offspring in the same round you get it. If you do, the offspring does not count as "newborn".
During scoring, you get 1 bonus point for each unfenced stable in your farmyard.
Pile (from bottom to top) 1 vegetable, reed, clay, wood, vegetable, stone, grain, reed on this card. At any time, you can buy the top good for 1 food.
Immediately after each time you use a wood accumulation space, you can exchange 1 wood for 2 food. If you do, place the wood on the accumulation space.
Each time you build at least 1 clay room or renovate your house from clay to stone, you also get 3 food.
Each time you build at least 1 room, you can place 1 food on each of the next 4 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
When you play this card, you immediately get 1 grain. In the field phase of each harvest, you can harvest 1 additional grain from each of your grain fields.
Each time you use the "Day Labourer" action space, you get 1 additional grain. From round 6 on, you can choose to get 1 vegetable instead.
Each time you use a wood accumulation space, you get 1 additional wood.
Immediately after each time you use the "Farmland", "Grain Seeds", "Grain Utilization", or "Cultivation" action space, you get 1 wood.
Once you no longer live in a wooden house, place 2 clay on each of the next 5 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the clay.
Each time you build a room/renovate, but only once per room/action, you can replace exactly 2 clay or 2 stone with 1 wood.
When you play this card, if you live in a clay house with exactly 2 rooms, you immediately get 3 clay, 2 reed, and 2 stone.
During the scoring, you get 2/3/4/5/7/9 bonus points for having at least 5/6/7/8/9/10 improvements in front of you.
Each time you use the "Fishing" accumulation space, you can also pay 1 wood to get 1 food for each person you have and 1 reed.
Every improvement, room, and renovation costs you 1 stone less.
Each time you use the "Sheep Market", "Pig Market", or "Cattle Market" accumulation space, you can buy 1 additional animal of the respective type for 1 food.
Each time you use the "Traveling Players" accumulation space, you get an additional 1 wood and 1 grain.
Each time another player uses the "Traveling Players" accumulation space, you get 1 food and 1 wood. Immediately after, you can buy exactly 1 vegetable for 2 food.
When you play this card, you immediately get 1 wild boar. Your wild boar breed at the end of round 12 (if there is room for the new wild boar).
Each time you use the "Day Laborer" action space, you can also either build exactly 1 room or renovate your house. Either way, you have to pay the cost.
When you play this card, you immediately get 1 wood. Once you live in a stone house, at the start of each round, you can build exactly 1 stable for 1 wood.
Each time you use the "Day Laborer" action space, you can also plow 1 field.
Each time you build a major improvement, reduce the stone cost by the number of rooms you have build onto your initial house.
Once you live in a stone house, at the start of each round, you can play an occupation for an occupation cost of 1 food, or a minor improvement (by paying its costs).
During scoring, you get 1 bonus point for each pasture containing at least 1 animal while having unused capacity for at least three more animals.
During scoring, you get 1 bonus point for each occupation played after this one.
When you play this card in a game with 1/2/3/4+ players, you immediately get 2 grain/3 clay/2 reed/2 sheep.
At any time, you can exchange 1 sheep in your farmyard for either 1 wild boar, 1 vegetable, or 1 stone.
Once you live in a stone house, place 3 food on each remaining round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
Each time you use a wood accumulation space, you get an additional "Bake Bread" action.
Immediately before playing each occupation after this one, you can pay 1 wood total to get 1 food for each occupation you have in front of you.
At the start of each round, if you have at least 3 rooms but only 2 people, you get 1 food and 1 crop of your choice (grain or vegetable).
As long as you live in a house with exactly 2 rooms, at the start of each round, you get 1 wood.
Each time you use the "Forest" or "Reed Bank" accumulation space, you also get 1 clay. In games with 3 or more players, this also applies to the "Clay Pit".
When you play this card, you can immediately pay 1 food. If you do, you get 1 stone for each room you have.
Every new room only costs you 3 of the appropriate building resource and 2 reed (e.g., if you live in a wooden house, 3 wood and 2 reed).
If there are still 1/3/6/9 complete rounds left to play, you immediately get 1/2/3/4 wood. During scoring, each player with the most rooms gets 3 bonus points.
Each time you use the "Grain Seeds" action space, you also get 1 vegetable.
Each time you renovate or build a room, you can replace the required 1 or 2 reed with a total of 1 wood.
Each time you use the "Resource Market" action space, you also get your choice of 1 clay or 1 grain.
Once you are the only player to live in a house with only 2 rooms, you immediately get 3 wood, 2 clay, 1 reed, and 1 stone (only once).
Add 2, 5, 8, and 10 to the current round and place 1 sheep on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you receive the sheep.
Each time you use the "Grain Seeds" action space, you can also buy 1 cattle for 1 food.

At any time, you may convert goods to Food as follows: Vegetable to 2 Food; Sheep to 2 Food; Wild boar to 2 Food; Cattle to 3 Food. Whenever you use the "Bake bread" action, you may convert: Grain to 2 Food.
card_rulings: You can own more than one Fireplace.

With this card, you can convert several animals and vegetables to food at the same time. When you bake bread you can convert as many grain as you want. You may bake bread and convert other goods to food at the same time, in order to activate the Gypsy's Crock.
Is a cooking improvement and a baking improvement, but not an oven.
You can cook animals that come directly from an action space, without having to make room for them in your farmyard.

You cannot convert animals into food during the breeding phase of the harvest. After the breeding phase of the last harvest, the game ends immediately; you can not convert the animals received in the last harvest to food at all.

card_text: At any time, you may convert goods to Food as follows: Vegetable to 2 Food; Sheep to 2 Food; Wild boar to 2 Food; Cattle to 3 Food. Whenever you use the "Bake bread" action, you may convert: Grain to 2 Food.
card_rulings: You can own more than one Fireplace.

With this card, you can convert several animals and vegetables to food at the same time. When you bake bread you can convert as many grain as you want. You may bake bread and convert other goods to food at the same time, in order to activate the Gypsy's Crock.
Is a cooking improvement and a baking improvement, but not an oven.
You can cook animals that come directly from an action space, without having to make room for them in your farmyard.

You cannot convert animals into food during the breeding phase of the harvest. After the breeding phase of the last harvest, the game ends immediately; you can not convert the animals received in the last harvest to food at all.

card_text: At any time, you may convert goods to Food as follows: Vegetable to 3 Food; Sheep to 2 Food; Wild boar to 3 Food; Cattle to 4 Food. Whenever you use the "Bake bread" action, you may convert: Grain to 3 Food.
card_rulings: If you upgrade the Fireplace major improvement, the card is returned to the major improvements board. If the minor improvement Simple Fireplace is upgraded, it is removed from the game.
You can own more than one Cooking Hearth.

With this card, you can convert several animals and vegetables to food at the same time. When you bake bread you can convert as many grain as you want. You may bake bread and convert other goods to food at the same time, in order to activate the Gypsy's Crock.
Is a cooking improvement and a baking improvement, but not an oven.
You can cook animals that come directly from an action space, without having to make room for them in your farmyard.

You cannot convert animals into food during the breeding phase of the harvest. After the breeding phase of the last harvest, the game ends immediately; you can not convert the animals received in the last harvest to food at all.

card_text: At any time, you may convert goods to Food as follows: Vegetable to 3 Food; Sheep to 2 Food; Wild boar to 3 Food; Cattle to 4 Food. Whenever you use the "Bake bread" action, you may convert: Grain to 3 Food.
card_rulings: If you upgrade the Fireplace major improvement, the card is returned to the major improvements board. If the minor improvement Simple FireplaceE20 is upgraded, it is removed from the game.
You can own more than one Cooking Hearth.

With this card, you can convert several animals and vegetables to food at the same time. When you bake bread you can convert as many grain as you want. You may bake bread and convert other goods to food at the same time, in order to activate the Gypsy's Crock.
Is a cooking improvement and a baking improvement, but not an oven.
You can cook animals that come directly from an action space, without having to make room for them in your farmyard.

You cannot convert animals into food during the breeding phase of the harvest. After the breeding phase of the last harvest, the game ends immediately; you can not convert the animals received in the last harvest to food at all.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Bake bread" Action, you can turn at most 1 Grain into 5 Food. When you take this card, you can immediately use the "Bake bread" Action.
card_rulings: When you play this card, you may bake bread with all of your ovens and other suitable improvements.
Is an oven.

card_text: Whenever you use the "Bake bread" Action, you can turn up to 2 Grain into 4 Food each. When you take this card, you can immediately use the "Bake bread" Action.
card_rulings: When you play this card, you may bake bread with all of your ovens and other suitable improvements.
Is an oven.

card_text: In each Harvest, you can use the Joinery to convert at most 1 Wood to 2 Food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 Bonus points for 3/5/7 Wood.
card_rulings: At the end of the game, wood on the Copse and the Forester counts.
Wood on the Basin Maker does not count.
Wood on the Resource Seller does not count.

card_text: In each Harvest, you can use the Pottery to convert at most 1 Clay to 2 Food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 Bonus points for 3/5/7 Clay.
In each Harvest, you can use the Basketmaker’s Workshop to convert at most 1 Reed to 3 Food. At the end of the game, you receive 1/2/3 Bonus points for 2/4/5 Reed.
Place 1 Food each on the next 5 Round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.
Immediately place 1 of your people.

Whenever you use the "Fishing" or "Take 1 Reed" Action space (on the second game board), you may later in the same round, pay 1 Food to place a person on this card. If you do this, you receive either 3 Stone or 1 Grain and 1 Vegetable.
Add 5,7, and 9 to the current Round and place 1 Vegetable on each corresponding Round space.  At the start of these Rounds, you receive the Vegetable.
Steal 2 Food markers from your left or right neighbor (your choice) and give them this card which they add to their hand. (*Rounds without Harvest are 1,2,3,5,6,8,10, and 12)

In a 4 player game, take the Action cards from the 5 player game and visa versa. At the beginning of each round, choose one randomly.  This Action space is available for you only that round. (Fill any Accumulation space once.)
Whenever any player uses the "Fishing" Action space, they may pay you 1 Food afterward for 1 Bonus point.
When you play this card, you receive 1 Grain. In each Field phase of Harvest, you may harvest 1 additional Grain from each of your Grain fields.

In Round 14, immediately before the Starting player places his first person, you may use 1 of your people to use all the Action spaces for Rounds 11,12,13 and 14 in any order. Leave your person on the last space you used. The Work phase is over for you. (You cannot place anymore people.)

Once you have built a Cooking Hearth, alternate placing 1 Food and 1 Grain on each remaining even numbered Round space. Start with 1 Food. At the start of these rounds, you receive the item. (If you already have a Cooking Hearth when you play this card, place the goods immediately.)
Whenever you use the "Day Laborer" Action space, you may also Plow 1 field.
Whenever you play another Minor Improvement, you receive 1 Grain afterward.
In the Returning Home phase after each Work phase where you gained at least 7 building resources, you receive 2 Food.
Pay 1 Wood less for all Major and Minor Improvements.
In each round from Round 5 on, the first player to place their second person on the same gameboard as their first person must first pay you 1 Food.

When you play this card, you receive 1 Reed and 1 Food. Instead of the "Build Room(s)" action on the corresponding Action space, you may take the "Renovate" action and vice versa. (This card does not effect additional actions on those spaces.)

When you Sow, you can plant Vegetables on this card as you would on a field. When you play this card, it starts with 2 Vegetables planted on it from the general supply. After those Vegetables are harvested, it can be Sowed again. (This card does not count as a field when scoring.)
In the Feeding phase of each Harvest, your people need only 1 Food each if the Food comes from Vegetables converted in that Feeding phase. (People who are not fed with Vegetables feed normally.)

The player on you left chooses 1 of your Unused farmyard spaces.  Place 1 Wood from the general supply on this space.  It counts as used and you can not build anything on it. (In a Solo game randomly choose a space.)

In the Replenish phase of each round (Phase 2), place 1 additional Food on the "Fishing" Action space. Whenever another player uses the "Fishing" Action space, they must pay you 2 Food from that Action space.

When you play this card, you may immediately buy 1 Sheep and/or 1 Cattle for 1 Food each. Whenever you end a round with the number of your Sheep plus twice the number of your Cattle greater than or equal to the current round number, you receive 1 Food. (If the current round ends with a Harvest, you may count your animals after the Harvest.)

Add 4,7, and 10 to the current Round and place 1 Guest token on each corresponding Round space. In those rounds, you may place the Guest token once as you would a person and return it after the Work phase. (The Guests do not need room in your home.)
When you play this card, pay 2 additional Food. Until the next Harvest, you may use Action spaces even if they are occupied by other players.

You receive 2 Food before you pay the costs of playing this Occupation. In the Returning home phase of each Round, you must choose 1 player among those who built or played a new card that round. That player receives 1 Food from the general supply. (You may choose yourself if possible.)
You immediately receive 2 Wood.

Immediately remove the person who played this Minor Improvement from the game completely.  This person does not occupy space in your home anymore. (You can only play this card using a Family member's action. The Cypress stands for death and mourning. *without guest)

Add 3 to the current round number and place the person who played this Occupation on the corresponding round space. (It is no longer considered part of your family until then.) At the start of that round, you receive the person back, even if you have no room in your home, and you receive 2 Food, 1 Bonus point, and 1 Vegetable.

Whenever you use the "Traveling Players" action on an Action space, you may move any person from an Action space with exactly 1 person on it to "Traveling players" also. (They don't use action.) Afterward, you may immediately use the freed Action space with another person.
If you use a "Family growth" action 1/2/3 rounds after playing this card, you also receive 2/3/5 Food. (Mark the 3rd round with an Arrow marker.)
This card provides room for 1 Family member.

Add 3 to the current round and place 12 of your fences on the corresponding Round space.  At the start of that round only, take your fences back and immediately take a "Fences" action without placing a Family member.  (Pay fence costs normally)
At the end of the game, you receive 1 and a half Bonus points for each room in your Stone house rounded down and your negative points are doubled. (This also applies to Begging cards.)
At the end of the game, if you have 2x3 or 3x2 connected Unused farmyard spaces, you receive 9 Bonus points. (Your 6 Unused spaces are still scored as 6 Unused spaces.)
Whenever another player uses the "Take 1 Sheep" Action space, you receive 1 Sheep from the general supply. (If you use the Action space, you do not receive a benefit.)

You may only play this Occupation when you have 3 or less resources and it is Round 11 or before. At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for each Family member. (Resources are Grain, Vegetables, and building resources. You may return building resources to the general supply for nothing at any time.)

You immediately receive 1 Grain and 1 Vegetable. The player on your left chooses 1 of your Unused farmyard spaces. Mark it with a green Arrow marker. This space must remain unused until all other spaces are used.
Whenever you Plow, you must pay 1 Food for each new field.
When you Sow, you can plant Vegetables between orthogonally adjacent field tiles as though the gaps were fields. (These gaps do not count as fields when scoring.)

Immediately place 4 Food from the general supply on 1 of your empty fields. In the Field phase of each Harvest, move 1 of these Food to your personal supply. You cannot Sow this field until it is empty again.

Whenever another player uses the "Fishing" Action space, you may move 1 of your already placed people to "Fishing" too and receive 1 Food from the general supply. (Thus, you free an Action space. You do not take the "Fishing" action.)

Any 2x2 Unused farmyard space, where 1 of the spaces is orthogonally adjacent to your home, provides room for 1 person. (Once one of the spaces becomes used, move the Family member back to your supply or your home if you have room.)
Whenever you use the "Day Laborer" Action space, you receive an additional 1 Wood and 1 Clay.

Whenever any player uses the "Fishing" Action space, you must receive 1 Bonus point. Afterward, if you still have at least 1 person in your home, you must skip placing a person on one of your turns in that round. (You still get to place the person on a later turn).
At the start of each round, randomly choose 1 player among the players with the least Food markers in their personal supply, to receive 1 Food.  If you have the least, then you receive the Food.
Whenever you play an Improvement and you would pay Stone as a building material, you may replace that Stone with the same amount of Clay if you pay an additional 1 Food.

At the beginning of each round (before Phase 1), if you have exactly 0-1/2-3/4-5/6+ Food in your personal supply, you receive 1 Wood/Clay/Reed/Stone. (You may give Food to the general supply at any time.)
When you play this card, you may immediately build 1 stable without paying Wood. You can keep up to 2 Sheep on exactly 1 Unused space in your farmyard. (The space still counts as unused.)
Whenever you use the "Plow 1 Field" or "Plow and/or Sow" Action space, you may take the Field phase of a Harvest on your farmyard before taking the action. (This does not count as a Harvest.)
When you play this card, you receive 2 Clay.  At any time you may convert 1 Clay into 1 Food.

Once during the game, when you use the "Plow 1 field" action, you may Plow 2 fields instead of 1.  Additionally, you can Sow these new fields (1 or 2), but only with Vegetables. (This does not apply to the "Plow and/or Sow" action.)
At the start of each Round, before Phase 1, you may pay 2 Food to immediately play an Occupation. (Only Occupations that do not require a person to be placed can be played this way.)

Whenever, at the start of a round before phase 1, another player has no Food markers, you may give them 1 Food from your personal supply. If you do this, you receive your choice of 1 Grain, 1 Wood, 1 Clay, 1 Reed, or 1 Sheep from the general supply.
At the end of each Harvest, you may pay 1 Wood and 1 Food to use out of turn an Action space of your choice without placing a person.
Whenever you use the "Fishing" Action space with exactly 3 Food on it, you receive an additional 1 Grain and 1 building resource of your choice.
Whenever you use 1 of the 6 leftmost Action spaces on the first game board, you receive 1 additional Wood.
Whenever you use the "Sow and/or Bake bread", "Plow 1 Field", or "Plow and/or Sow" Action spaces, you may buy 1 Grain for 1 Food before taking the action.
Immediately harvest 1 Grain from each of your Grain fields.

This card is an additional Action space for you only. When you use this Action space the 1st/ 2nd/ 3rd/ 4th time, you receive 1 Begging card and 6/7/8/9 different Goods of your choice. (Goods are Grain, Vegetables, building resources and animals.)

Immediately after each Work phase, you may pay 1 Food to place 1 of your people on the Joy House instead of your home. Whenever you use a person from the Joy House, you may pay 1 Food to use 2 vertically adjacent Action spaces. Use the top one first then the bottom one. (People in the Joy House also use a room in your home)

Whenever you use your second Family member to use the "Family growth" Action space in Stage 2, you may immediately afterward move it and use another Action space. The "Family growth" Action space remains occupied by your newborn.
Whenever you start a round in a Wooden Hut, you receive 1 Food.
In the Breeding phase of each Harvest, if you breed Sheep/ Wild boar/ Cattle and you have exactly 0-3/4-7/8+ planted Grain, you may receive 1 additional baby Sheep/ Wild Boar/ Cattle.
Whenever you use the "Bake bread" action in a round immediately following a Harvest, you receive 2 additional Food.

At the end of each round that does not end with a Harvest, the player with the most dairy animals receives 1 Grain. (Dairy animals are Sheep and Cattle). Cattle count double in this calculation. If you tie for the most, only you receive the Grain. If two other players tie or no one has dairy animals, no one receives the Grain.
Whenever you use the "Fishing" Action space, you may take up to 2 different building resources that you do not have in your personal supply.
Whenever you take Wood/ Clay/ Reed/ Sheep/ Wild Boar/ Food from an Accumulation space, you may convert exactly 1 of them into 1 Clay/ Reed/ Stone/ Wild Boar/ Cattle/ Grain (respectively).
Whenever you use the "Take 3 Wood", "Take 1 Clay", or "Take 1 Reed" Action spaces located above the "Fishing" Action space, you receive 1 additional Food.
Whenever you use the Basketmaker's Workshop in a Harvest, you receive 2 additional Grain. For this, you must have at least 3 Family members. (Guests are not counted.)
You immediately receive 1 Wood for every Unused farmyard space orthogonally adjacent to your home.

Each round, before the Work phase, you may mark 3 Action spaces. At most 2 of these spaces can be used by others. The third must remain free for you (even if you do not have any people left). You may choose not to use the third space.
Whenever you use the "Fishing" Action space or one of its neighbors, "Day Laborer", "Take 1 Reed", or the Round 4 Action space, you receive 1 additional Reed.
You immediately receive 1 Stone.
At any time, any player can use the Gauger to convert 1 Grain into 2 Food. Whenever another player uses the Gauger to convert 1 or more Grain into Food, you receive 1 Food from the general supply.

In the round you play this card, all Action spaces count as unoccupied for you. (Even if one of your people use it.) From now, Action spaces that are used by another player's last person count as unoccupied for you.
Between the next Harvest and the Harvest after that, each other player must pay 1 Food to the general supply at the start of each Work phase. If they cannot pay 1 Food, they receive 1 negative point.

The Night Watch is an additional Action space for all. In the Replenish phase of each round, add 1 Grain to this card. A player who uses this action must pay 2 Food to the general supply and receives the Grain. Some or all of this Grain may be immediately exchanged for Bonus points, 1 for 1.

Place 2 Vegetables from the general supply on this card.  Whenever you Plow 1 field, you may take 1 Vegetable from this card.  Whenever you use the "Take 1 Grain" Action space, you may place 1 Vegetable from the general supply on this card.
Randomly choose 1 of your Unused farmyard spaces. Rules permitting, you may either fence this space or build a stable there without paying Wood. Alternatively, you may Plow 1 field there.
When you play this card, you receive 3 Food. For each Vegetable that you receive from your fields in the Field phase of Harvest, you receive 1 additional Food.
Alternate placing 1 Food and 1 Sheep on each remaining Round space. Start with 1 Food.  At the start of these rounds you receive the item.
Immediately change 1 room at no cost into a Stone room. (You will never need to renovate this room. Your home is still considered the material of your other rooms.)

When you play this card, you receive 1 Wood. Whenever you receive 3 or more items of the same type (excluding Wood) from an Accumulation space, you receive 1 Wood. (Accumulation spaces are Action spaces where Goods or Food accumulate.)
Once each round, whenever you have no Food in your personal supply, you receive 1 Food.
At the end of each Work phase, you may take a Begging card.  If you do, you may immediately receive 3 Food and place a Guest token.  Afterward, return the Guest token.

Whenever you use the "Fishing" Action space or one of its neighbors ("Day Laborer", "Take 1 Reed", or the Round 4 Action space), you receive 1 additional Food for each person occupying any of these 4 spaces excluding the person you just placed.
At the end of the game, you receive 1 Bonus point for each Unused farmyard space diagonally or orthogonally adjacent to your home. (These spaces still count as unused when scoring.)

Place 2 Wood from the general supply on this card.  Whenever you take Wood from an Accumulation space, this card gets 1 additional Wood.  Whenever you pay at least 1 Wood with a person's action, you may use 1 Wood from this card as payment.

Whenever you use a Stage 1 Action space (Round 1 to 4 spaces), you may reveal the next round card and place it in front of you.  You and only you can use it this round. (At the start of next round, return the Action space to the board and all players use it normally.)

Whenever another player uses the "Traveling Players" action, they must pay you 1 Food and you receive 1 additional Food from the general supply. (You receive no benefit if you use the "Traveling Players" action.)
Whenever you use the "Fishing" Action space, place 1 Food on the next 3 Round spaces.  At the start of these rounds, you receive the Food.

In the Breeding phase of each Harvest, you receive half a Food from the general supply for each other player who has at least 1 Sheep, rounded up. Also, you receive 1 Food for each other player who has at least 1 Cattle. (Your animals do not count.)
When you play this card, you may immediately build up to 2 Major Improvements by paying 1 Food each and discarding your entire hand. (You do not need to pay the costs of the Major Improvements)

Once you have built a Clay hut, place 1 Grain on each remaining even numbered Round space.  At the start of these rounds, you receive the Grain. (If you already have a Clay hut or Stone house when you play this card, place the Grain immediately.)
When you play this card, immediately pay 1 Clay and 1 Food for each of your Family members. (Guests are not counted.)

When you play this card, you receive 1 Wood, 1 building resource of your choice, 1 Sheep, 1 Wild Boar, 1 Food, 1 Grain and 1 Vegetable. Also, you may receive 2 Bonus points. In the Returning home phase of this round, remove 1 of your Family members from the game completely.

Whenever you take Stone from a Stone Accumulation space, you may take 1 Stone only and leave any remaining Stone. Place the 1 Stone on this card. The Stone cost of all Improvements is reduced by the number of Stone on this card for you.
Whenever you play an Improvement (including this one), you receive 1 Food afterward.
Whenever another player takes 1 to 3 Wood from a Wood Accumulation space with a person, you receive 1 Wood from the general supply. (When you take Wood, you receive no benefit.)
If you have a Cooking Improvement, you may convert 1 Grain into 2 Food at any time.

Place 1 Grain, 1 Vegetable, 1 Sheep, 1 Wild Boar, 1 Cattle, 1 Wood, 1 Clay, 1 Reed, and 1 Stone on this card.  At the start of each round, before Phase 1, you randomly take 1 good. (Goods on this card at the end of the game do nothing.)

Once each round that ends with a Harvest, immediately after placing 1 of your people, you may place 1 Food from the general supply on any Action space. The next player to use this Action space receives the Food (even in a following round).
